PEGGIOR VIAGGIO DELLA MIA VITA A causa di overbooking del volo Jeddah- Jakarta del 25 Luglio 2025 io ed altre persone non abbiamo potuto prendere il volo, rimanendo bloccati a Jeddah per tutta la notte.
Lo staff di Saudia nell’aeroporto di Jeddah ci ha indirizzato in un volo del giorno dopo (e ci ha assicurato che avremmo avuto un rimborso del biglietto della tratta Jeddah-Jakarta di almeno il 150%), ma a causa del gravissimo ritardo, abbiamo perso la coincidenza del volo che avremmo avuto successivamente, Jakarta-Denpasar a causa del loro disguido e abbiamo dovuto acquistare un nuovo biglietto, (dal costo di 104€), e abbiamo dovuto trovarci un hotel a nostre spese per la notte a Jakarta, ci hanno garantito che anche questi sarebbero stati rimborsati.
Di conseguenza abbiamo perso ANCHE una notte già pagata nella casa prenotata a Bali.
A fine vacanza abbiamo scritto alla compagnia utilizzando il form indicato sul loro sito, come suggerito dai dipendenti di suadia, che prometteva una risposta entro tot settimane. Sono passati mesi e ancora NESSUNA risposta, servizio clienti TERRIBILE, se chiamate vi dicono che non sanno niente e l'unica cosa che si può fare è aspettare che rispondano via mail (cosa che non fanno).
In sintesi: volo spostato di un giorno intero, coincidenza persa, hotel a nostre spese, aerei vecchi e spesso sedili con vari problemi, E NESSUN RIMBORSO nonostante le promesse fatte.
PEGGIOR VIAGGIO DELLA MIA VITA, NON USATE QUESTA COMPAGNIA.
translated_text: WORST TRIP OF MY LIFE Due to overbooking of the Jeddah-Jakarta flight on July 25, 2025, I and other people could not take the flight, being stuck in Jeddah all night. Saudia staff at Jeddah airport directed us to a flight the next day (and assured us we would get a refund of at least 150% of the Jeddah-Jakarta ticket), but due to the severe delay, we missed the connecting flight Jakarta-Denpasar because of their mistake and had to buy a new ticket (costing 104€), and we had to find a hotel at our own expense for the night in Jakarta, which they guaranteed would also be reimbursed. Consequently, we also lost a night already paid for in the house booked in Bali. At the end of the vacation, we wrote to the company using the form on their website, as suggested by Saudia employees, which promised a response within a few weeks. Months have passed and still NO response, TERRIBLE customer service, if you call they say they know nothing and the only thing you can do is wait for an email reply (which they never send). In summary: flight delayed by a whole day, missed connection, hotel at our expense, old planes and often seats with various problems, AND NO REFUND despite promises made. WORST TRIP OF MY LIFE, DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY.

إحباط كبير عندما حجزت تذكرتي على الخطوط السعودية، بصفتها الناقل الوطني لدولة خليجية مرموقة، لم يتسلل إلى ذهني للحظة أنني سأعيش تجربة بهذا القدر من السوء. لقد اضطررت لحجز رحلة (عمان - جدة) عبر الرياض (ترانزيت)، وكانت النتيجة سلسلة من الإخفاقات التي لا تُغتفر:
• تخبط المواعيد: قامت الشركة بتغيير موعد الرحلة، مما أدى لتأخري ساعة كاملة عن الوصول لجدة، وهو ما ترتب عليه إلغاء حجز السيارة التي كانت بانتظاري لنقلي إلى مكة المكرمة.
• معاناة الترانزيت: رغم أن حجزي "ترانزيت"، إلا أنني صُدمت في الرياض بضرورة ختم دخول السعودية، واستلام الحقائب، ثم استقلال حافلة لمدة 20 دقيقة للوصول إلى صالة الرحلات الداخلية والقيام بإجراءات التسجيل (check-in) من جديد! هذا التخبط جعلني في حالة ركض مستمر وضغط عصبي بسبب ضيق الوقت.
• الاستهتار بحقوق الركاب: تم تغيير مقعدي في رحلة الرياض-جدة رغم أنه مقعد مدفوع ثمنه مسبقاً! وعندما احتججت، كان رد الموظفة الواهي: "ربما المقعد مكسور!".. كيف لشركة كبرى أن تغير مقعداً مدفوعاً بذرائع غير منطقية؟!
• غياب الخدمات اللوجستية: عند وصولنا الرياض، نزلنا عبر السلالم إلى حافلة استغرقت 10 دقائق لتوصيلنا للمطار! أين الممرات والخراطيم المباشرة؟ هل تُركت للطيران الاقتصادي وحُرم منها ركاب الناقل الوطني؟!
• أسطول متهالك: الطائرات قديمة جداً وتفتقر لأدنى معايير الترفيه؛ فلا شاشات ولا حتى خدمة ترفيه عبر الهاتف. شعرت في رحلتي (عمان-الرياض) و(جدة-عمان) أنني عدت بالزمن إلى طائرات التسعينات.
• وجبات هزيلة: قائمة الطعام لا تليق أبداً باسم "السعودية"؛ فالاختيار محصور بين قطعة "بيتزا مارغريتا" صغيرة أو "مافن"! هل يعقل أن تكون هذه وجبة رحلة مدتها ساعتان ونصف بين عاصمتين؟ هذا المستوى لا نراه حتى في أفقر شركات الطيران الاقتصادي.
• فوضى بوابات الصعود: في رحلة العودة بجدة، تم تغيير بوابة الصعود فجأة وأنا في السوق الحرة إلى أبعد بوابة في المطار (15 دقيقة مشياً)، مما تسبب في حالة انزعاج ومشي سريع باتجاه البوابة الجديدة البعيدة.
• تدني مستوى النظافة: بدلاً من أغطية الرأس القماشية التي تُبدل لكل راكب، تستخدم الشركة أغطية جلدية ثابتة ومقززة تفتقر لأدنى معايير التعقيم والنظافة.
• ضعف الكفاءات: لغة مسؤول طاقم الطائرة والطيار الإنجليزية (عبر الإذاعة الداخلية) ضعيفة جداً ومليئة بأخطاء ساذجة لا تليق بموظفي ناقل وطني يمثل المملكة أمام العالم.
حقيقة هي تجربة لا ترقى حتى لمستوى الطيران "منخفض التكاليف"، ولن أكرر تجربة السفر عبر هذه الخطوط مجدداً ما دام هذا هو مستوى خدماتها.
translated_text: Great frustration when I booked my ticket on Saudi Airlines, as the national carrier of a prestigious Gulf country, it never crossed my mind that I would have such a bad experience. I had to book a flight (Amman - Jeddah) via Riyadh (transit), and the result was a series of unforgivable failures: • Schedule confusion: The company changed the flight time, causing me to arrive an hour late in Jeddah, which led to the cancellation of the car reservation that was waiting to take me to Mecca. • Transit suffering: Although my booking was 'transit', I was shocked in Riyadh to have to stamp entry into Saudi Arabia, collect luggage, then take a 20-minute bus to the domestic terminal and check in again! This confusion made me run continuously and caused nervous stress due to tight timing. • Disregard for passenger rights: My seat on the Riyadh-Jeddah flight was changed even though it was prepaid! When I complained, the staff's weak response was: 'Maybe the seat is broken!' How can a major company change a paid seat with illogical excuses?! • Lack of logistics: Upon arrival in Riyadh, we descended via stairs to a bus that took 10 minutes to reach the airport! Where are the direct corridors and jet bridges? Are they left only for budget airlines and denied to the national carrier's passengers?! • Worn-out fleet: The planes are very old and lack basic entertainment standards; no screens or even phone entertainment service. I felt on my flights (Amman-Riyadh) and (Jeddah-Amman) like I went back to the 90s planes. • Meager meals: The menu is not worthy of the name 'Saudi'; the choice was limited to a small 'Margherita pizza' or a 'muffin'! Is this a meal for a two and a half hour flight between capitals? This level is not even seen in the poorest budget airlines. • Boarding gate chaos: On the return flight in Jeddah, the boarding gate was suddenly changed while I was in the duty-free to the farthest gate in the airport (15 minutes walk), causing discomfort and a fast walk to the new distant gate. • Poor cleanliness: Instead of cloth headrest covers changed for each passenger, the company uses fixed leather covers that are disgusting and lack basic sterilization and cleanliness standards. • Weak competencies: The English of the cabin crew leader and pilot (via internal radio) is very poor and full of silly mistakes unworthy of employees of a national carrier representing the Kingdom to the world. Honestly, this experience does not even reach the level of 'low-cost' flying, and I will not repeat traveling with this airline as long as this is their service level.

raw_text:
Review of Saudi Airlines Unfortunately, my experience at Jeddah Airport was extremely disappointing due to consistently poor service and unprofessional, condescending attitudes from staff.

I have travelled through many international airports around the world, and the level of rudeness and lack of professionalism displayed here stood out in the worst possible way. From the very beginning at the baggage check-in area, which was part of Saudi Airlines operations, the female staff responsible for weighing luggage were dismissive, rude, and spoke in a condescending manner, with no patience or courtesy shown.

The situation was made even more distressing as I was travelling with two elderly family members both grandmothers who required assistance. The staff assisting with luggage and wheelchairs were extremely rude, made inappropriate comments, and created an uncomfortable and unpleasant environment. Elderly passengers deserve care, respect, and compassion, none of which were shown.

Instead of feeling supported and welcomed, the overall atmosphere felt hostile and unnecessarily stressful. It is genuinely sad that an airport of this scale and importance, in partnership with a national airline, offers such poor service and displays such a lack of basic customer care.

An international airport especially one serving millions of pilgrims, families, and travellers each year should uphold far higher standards. This experience was unacceptable and has left a very negative impression. I sincerely hope both airport management and Saudi Airlines take staff conduct, training, and accountability seriously so that no other travellers, particularly the elderly, are subjected to this kind of treatment.

raw_text: Avoid using Saudia Airlines, they do not care about their customers Really disappointed with the service received by the Saudia Airlines last week. On Thursday 1st January, our 8.30am flight SV101 eventually got cancelled after waiting 3 hours on the plane. Most of the Saudia airport staff were unhelpful and very dismissive. All we wanted to know was what was going on as I was traveling with 2 young children and was in the airport from 5am with no sleep. Eventually we were taken to a hotel and was told that the coach would pick us up the next day at 4am to take us back to the airport for our rescheduled 9am flight. The coach did turn up and took the 1st batch off passengers but did not return to pick the rest of us up (approximately 50 passengers). We waited and waited and eventually had no choice but to arrange our own transport, causing more anxiety and stress. It was my our first time flying with Saudia and I was really shocked and disappointed with the level of service. We won't be traveling with them again anytime soon. I would advise people to use other airlines like Qatar Airways if possible.

Refused Check-in Despite Arriving Before Departure My mother and little sister were traveling on a domestic Saudi Airlines flight from Jeddah to Dammam. Because of heavy traffic, they arrived at the airport around 40 minutes before departure.

The check-in agent refused to accept them and said that “everything is closed.” The way they were treated was rude and unprofessional, and no real help or solution was offered. They were left stressed and confused, and they missed their flight even though they arrived before departure time.

To make matters worse, the online check-in was not working on the Saudi Airlines website, so there was no way to check in earlier.

raw_text:
Half the Flight Without Luggage, No Support from Flynas Extremely poor baggage handling, zero accountability, and ineffective customer service. Travel with caution.

I travelled with Flynas on 3 January 2026 from Cairo to Kuwait via Jeddah (Flights XY-566 and XY-529). Two checked bags did not arrive, and despite filing an official PIR at Kuwait Airport, there was no meaningful update, no timeline, and no proactive communication for over 24 hours.

What made this experience unacceptable was not just the missing baggage, but the complete failure of customer service and the ground staff responsible for baggage handling and delivery. There was no coordination, no ownership, and no clear point of contact. Passengers are left to chase information endlessly.

Mishandled baggage can happen — but silence, poor communication, and lack of responsibility should not. Based on this experience, I strongly advise travellers to think twice before flying Flynas, especially if you are checking in luggage.

Definitely recommend When I first booked my holiday, I hadn't actually looked into or heard of Saudia so when I then looked up their reviews, I was hugely worried and nearly cancelled the tickets as I was expecting it to be awful

We flew from London to Dubai, with a layover in Jeddah and Riyadh on the respective legs

We did have two minor delays but other than that, found the whole process at the airports very smooth, the planes were clean and comfortable, staff were friendly and the food was plentiful and tasty

I did need to speak to Customer Services before travelling and also found them very helpful - though would recommend calling as their website is quite glitchy

3rd January my flight was canceled from Berlin to Jeddah, after being in the aircraft for 4hours.

Despite the fact that a wheelchair had already been pre-booked, she was denied proper wheelchair assistance. She had a 10 cm cut on her foot with stitches done just the day before, and was clearly in pain. raw_text:
Severe Service Failure and Total Lack of Accountability Date: 6 January 2026
Me leaving the customer service: 7:22 PM

This message is to formally report a serious operational failure and gross misconduct at the customer service counter on 6 January 2026.

A system failure in the Flynas app prevented online check-in and the purchase of additional baggage. Instead of resolving a problem caused by Flynas’ own platform, the counter staff chose to exploit the situation, forcing payment at an inflated airport rate with no justification or support.

The counter agents demonstrated complete incompetence and indifference. They were unable—or unwilling—to assist, take ownership, or apply basic problem-solving. When escalation was requested, a supervisor was supposedly contacted. I waited over 30 minutes, yet no manager ever appeared. This is not an oversight; it is a deliberate disregard for passengers.

The behavior displayed was dismissive, evasive, and disrespectful. There was no attempt to explain, correct, or even acknowledge the failure. Leaving a passenger stranded without leadership support is unacceptable and indefensible.

This incident reflects poor training, weak supervision, and a broken service culture. An airline that tolerates this level of negligence is actively damaging its own reputation.

This is not a complaint seeking sympathy—it is a demand for accountability. Immediate review of the counter team and on-duty management is warranted. Failure to address such conduct signals that Flynas accepts operational chaos and customer mistreatment as standard practice.

raw_text: Terrible experience with Saudia . We had our trip interrupted due to denied boarding on our Rome Fiumicino-Nairobi flight, with a stopover in Jeddah, at 3:10 PM on December 21, 2025, operated by Saudia, despite having been to the gate on time (before all other passengers) and having a valid passport and visa issued by the Kenyan authorities. The visa was requested at the end of September, specifying the travel dates on which we were supposed to be in Kenya (December 22-December 28, 2025). These dates are listed on the document itself, issued on September 24, 2025, valid for 90 days and renewable for another 90 days upon arrival in Nairobi. We also requested a Denied Boarding Statement from Saudia, as we are entitled to request under EC Regulation 261/2004, but it was denied. During all this, we contacted the Kenyan immigration office, and they promptly responded on the official number +................ confirming that the visa was still valid on December 22nd, the day after our flight. Therefore, we believe it is right to give Saudia Airlines a negative review for their terrible and unprofessional handling of passengers, who deserve a little respect considering they lost not only the money for their flights but also their vacation.

raw_text:
⭐️ ABSOLUTELY AVOID SAUDIA GROUND STAFF: A Tale of Disastrous Service, Visa Denial, and Delayed Baggage (Flight Ref: 92H The ONLY Positive: Excellent In-Flight Experience
It is important to state clearly: my experience onboard Saudia's flights was top-notch.

Cabin and Comfort: The flights were clean, the seats were good, and the overall flight quality was high.

Service: The food was great, and the in-flight staff and pilots were excellent, professional, and attentive.

However, this positive experience was completely and utterly ruined by the unprofessional, incompetent, and frankly depressing service provided by Saudia's ground staff at both Jeddah and London, leading to significant financial loss and distress during my Umrah trip.

Issue 1: Stopover Visa SCAM & Financial Loss at Jeddah
I specifically booked with Saudia because of the highly advertised Stopover Visa Programme and I had paid for the associated insurance.

Misinformation: My initial check-in assured me the visa would be processed in Jeddah.

Ground Staff Failure: Upon arrival in Jeddah, the ground staff was clueless about their own airline's flagship visa programme.

Rude Supervisor: I was forced to wait 30-40 minutes for a supervisor who was shockingly rude. He flatly denied the existence of the Stopover Visa, refused to look at my evidence, and said his job was only for check-ins and upgrades.

Result: Due to this incompetence and lack of customer support, I was forced to pay again for a 1-year visa at immigration just for a 3-day Umrah visit, costing me money for the new visa, my family's parking, and precious time.

Issue 2: Racist and Rude Check-in at Jeddah (Jeddah to Dubai)
The service for my connection flight was equally appalling and felt discriminatory.

Excessive Wait: Despite Jeddah being Saudia's hub, the economy check-in line took over 1.5 hours due to inadequate staffing (only two counters open).

Prejudiced Agent: The check-in agent was immediately rude, impatient, and denied me check-in after seeing my Indian passport, demanding a UAE visa or residency.

Denial of Eligibility: He refused to check my passport or listen to my explanation that I was eligible for a Visa On Arrival due to possessing a valid US Visa and UK Residency. His conduct was discriminatory and completely unprofessional.

Resolution: The issue only resolved after a kind supervisor intervened and had to personally educate his staff on basic visa policies for eligible passengers.

Issue 3: Unannounced Baggage Delay and Lack of Care (Dubai to London)
My disastrous experience concluded with a major operational failure in London.

Zero Communication: I waited an hour at baggage collection, only to be told after everyone else had left that my bag was delayed due to "technical reasons"—a fact no one informed me of beforehand.

Distress & Health Risk: My bag contained essential clothing and medication, leaving me stranded in London empty-handed.

Customer Service Lie: The help desk initially promised evening delivery, only to suddenly change her story and say nothing would happen until after Monday because it was a weekend and no staff were working. She simply told me to call the airline.

Conclusion: A Pathetic Ground Experience
Saudia Airlines needs to understand that a great flight is worthless if the ground service is a hostile, disorganized mess. They failed to honor a paid-for service, their staff is untrained, rude, and potentially discriminatory, and their baggage management is incompetent.

Until Saudia radically overhauls its ground operations and customer service training, I strongly advise all travellers to avoid them.

raw_text:
Pathetic Experience My experience with Saudi Airlines on both inbound and outbound flights between Mumbai and Jeddah was extremely upsetting and unacceptable. The airline staff displayed rude, arrogant, and racially insensitive behaviour, especially towards passengers who required wheelchair assistance.

Despite requesting a wheelchair for an elderly passenger well in advance, no assistance was provided. The ground staff spoke in a condescending and dismissive manner, showing zero empathy or professionalism. Elderly passengers were left standing, confused, and helpless, while staff members responded with irritation instead of support.

What is even more disturbing is that this was not an isolated incident. The same rude attitude was shown to all passengers needing wheelchair assistance, clearly indicating a systemic problem and lack of respect for elderly and disabled travellers.

An international airline operating on religious and humanitarian values should be ashamed of such treatment. This behaviour reflects poor training, lack of accountability, and an underlying sense of discrimination. Passengers are not doing a favour by flying with your airline—they are paying customers who deserve dignity and basic human respect.

I strongly urge Saudi Airlines to:

Properly train staff in basic courtesy and sensitivity

Ensure wheelchair assistance is reliably provided

Investigate discriminatory behaviour on the Mumbai–Jeddah route

Katastrophale Airline – völliges Versagen auf ganzer Linie Diese Airline hat in jeder Hinsicht versagt. So eine schlechte und respektlose Erfahrung habe ich noch nie gemacht.
Unser Gepäck wurde beschädigt ausgeliefert. Der Umgang damit war vollkommen gleichgültig und unprofessionell. Keine Verantwortung, keine Lösung, kein Interesse. Man wird schlicht allein gelassen.
Der Service an Bord war ein Desaster. Uns wurde Essen serviert, das wir ausdrücklich nicht bestellt hatten. Als wir das angesprochen haben, hieß es lediglich, man könne nichts machen. Für eine Airline ist das absolut inakzeptabel und zeigt ein komplettes Fehlen von Serviceverständnis.
Das Verhalten der Stewardessen war besonders schockierend. Unprofessionell, unaufmerksam und respektlos. Gespräche mit Passagieren fanden statt, während sie selbst mit vollem Mund aßen. So ein Auftreten ist für den Kundenkontakt schlicht untragbar.
Insgesamt herrschte Chaos, Desinteresse und fehlende Kompetenz. Kein Service, keine Problemlösung, kein professionelles Auftreten. Diese Airline ist aus meiner Sicht nicht empfehlenswert und wird von mir definitiv nie wieder gebucht.
translated_text:
Catastrophic airline – complete failure across the board
This airline failed in every respect. I have never had such a bad and disrespectful experience.
Our luggage was delivered damaged. The handling was completely indifferent and unprofessional. No responsibility, no solution, no interest. You are simply left alone.
The service on board was a disaster. We were served food that we explicitly did not order. When we mentioned this, they just said nothing could be done. For an airline, this is absolutely unacceptable and shows a complete lack of service understanding.
The behavior of the flight attendants was particularly shocking. Unprofessional, inattentive, and disrespectful. Conversations with passengers took place while they themselves ate with full mouths. Such behavior is simply unacceptable for customer contact.
Overall, there was chaos, disinterest, and lack of competence. No service, no problem-solving, no professional appearance. In my view, this airline is not recommended and I will definitely never book it again.

raw_text:
Sub-par customer service and lack of maintenance The Upgrade Nightmare

I successfully bid for a Business Class upgrade, received the invoice, and was charged. However, the technical integration was non-existent. My online check-in and seat selection continued to show Economy.

Pre-Flight Support: I contacted customer service multiple times 24 hours before departure. Each time, I was met with indifference. Agents refused to verify my status, repeatedly telling me I’d have to "find out at airport check-in."

The Layover Struggle: Even at the airport, the staff was unhelpful. Instead of resolving the clear discrepancy in their system, they prompted me to file a complaint form rather than looking into the issue. It took significant "convincing" and a hard-fought argument to get them to actually check my booking.

The Result: While I eventually got the seat I had already paid for, the process left me stressed and worried throughout my entire trip. A premium passenger should never have to fight this hard for a confirmed service.

Maintenance also seems to be an issue. On my grandmother's return flight, her Business Class seat had a broken tray, which is a basic oversight that shouldn't happen in a premium cabin, especially when traveling as a senior.

My issues aren't isolated to this flight. I have previously dealt with double-charging on a booking, and the recovery process was a bureaucratic black hole:

I visited the Riyadh office, called the service center, and filed online forms.
Every agent promised a resolution "in a few days," yet no one ever followed
The refund was never processed, despite months of chasing them through every available channel (sales team, online forms, and in-person visits).

Saudia’s ground staff and support agents seem dismissive and eager to redirect you to an online form rather than solving the problem in front of them. Unlike other leading airlines that prioritize passenger peace of mind, Saudia feels like a "file a ticket and hope for the best" operation. Until they fix their backend systems and train their staff to actually care about resolution, I would be hesitant to rely on them for high-stakes travel.

The worst airline experiences بالطبع، إليك نموذج لمراجعة باللغة الإنجليزية تعبر عن تجربتك مع طيران ناس:

---

"I had a disappointing experience with Flynas. Upon arriving at the airport, I was informed that my flight schedule had been changed, and this happened not once, but twice. I was traveling on a transit flight, and these changes significantly affected my plans.

This has been one of the worst airline experiences I have encountered. Due to the changes in the flight schedule, my employer deducted a week’s salary because of the delay, which affected me both financially and emotionally. My vacation was supposed to be a break from work obligations, but it turned into a stressful situation.

I hope the airline can improve its services and communication in the future."

raw_text:
THE HELL OF AN BAGGAGE SERVICE THE HELL WITH THIS AIRLINE. I am extremely disappointed with Flynas for their mishandling of my baggage while traveling from Madinah (MED) to Doha (DOH) after completing my Umrah.

My luggage did not arrive with me and was delayed for an entire day. After a long and spiritually meaningful journey, the last thing any passenger expects is the stress of missing baggage. Unfortunately, Flynas showed poor communication and very little urgency in resolving the issue. I was left without my belongings, essentials, and proper updates during this time.

While I did eventually receive my luggage after one day, the inconvenience, anxiety, and frustration caused cannot be ignored. An airline should handle baggage responsibly, especially on routes carrying pilgrims who are already exhausted from travel.

This experience has seriously affected my trust in Flynas. I expect better service, accountability, and compensation for the inconvenience caused. I would advise travelers to be cautious when choosing this airline.

raw_text:
Refund and EC261 compensation wrongly refused – unacceptable handling Our flight SV210 from Milan Malpensa to Jeddah on 4 December 2025 was cancelled after boarding due to a technical issue. As a result, we missed our connecting flight to Jakarta (SV818), both flights being part of the same booking.

No alternative flight was offered and no refund was provided, in clear violation of Regulation (EC) No 261/2004.

Over several weeks, our claim was wrongly rejected, with responsibilities endlessly shifted between Guest Relations and Back Office. We were repeatedly told to “wait” or open new tickets. No one took ownership of the case.

Saudia repeatedly claimed the cancellation was due to “circumstances beyond the airline’s control,” which is factually incorrect: a technical issue is not an extraordinary circumstance under EC261.

Even the ticket refund has still not been processed, with illogical excuses provided each time. We are now forced to escalate the matter to the Italian Civil Aviation Authority (ENAC) and pursue legal action to recover both the refund and the €600 compensation per passenger.

Extremely frustrating and unprofessional experience. I strongly advise other passengers to be cautious regarding post-disruption support and respect of passenger rights.

raw_text:
Complaint Against Saudi Airlines Representative Khurram Khalid for Bribery and Abuse of Authority I missed my flight on 18 January, 2026 from Lahore to Jeddah and onward to London due to the unprofessional and unacceptable conduct of a senior representative of Saudi Arabian Airlines named Khurram Khalid.

I waited for more than three hours in long queues, first outside and then inside the airport. During this time, junior staff members, along with the senior representative Khurram Khalid, repeatedly asked me for money to process my check-in faster. They said in my local language, “kuch ley dey k baat khatam kren”. I did not have money to give them, so I refused.

When it was finally my turn at the counter and the counter staff asked for my passport, Khurram Khalid singled me out from the queue and instructed the counter staff to return my passport. This entire situation was deliberately created approximately one hour before my scheduled departure, at a time when I still had sufficient time to check in and board the flight.

I politely requested him and followed him across the floor, asking calmly why I was not being allowed to check in while all other passengers were being processed. He behaved in an egoistic and aggressive manner, clearly misusing his authority because he did not get what he wanted.

I then went to another counter where the staff again took my passport to check me in, but once more Khurram Khalid intervened, scolded the counter staff, and ordered him to return my passport. The counter staff had no choice but to obey and proceeded to check in the passenger behind me instead. I was told that only Khurram Khalid could resolve the issue, so I approached him again.

At this point, Khurram Khalid took my photograph without my consent and threatened me. This triggered me to start recording a video as evidence of how I was deliberately singled out, prevented from checking in, and caused to miss my flight. In the video, it can be clearly seen that the counter staff was willing to process my check-in, but the senior representative repeatedly stopped them and forced me to follow him around the airport to satisfy his ego.

He created unnecessary drama, and when I raised my voice in frustration, he reacted negatively. The most alarming part is that Khurram Khalid was carrying an expired ID card while exercising authority.

Terrible experience. Saudia Airlines is definitely not recommended. Terrible experience with Saudia Airlines. After boarding our flight from Milan to Jakarta (via Jeddah), we were informed of a technical issue and a short delay. Instead, we were kept on board for over three hours with no assistance or information, then disembarked and sent to the arrivals area.
At arrivals, there was no Saudia Airlines representative to assist passengers, only airport staff, who could not help with rebooking or information.
Because of this situation, I missed my connecting flight and received no communication, rebooking, assistance, or refund. I had to buy a new ticket with another airline to reach my destination.
Customer service is non-existent: multiple attempts to contact the airline were ignored, and my complaint was closed as “beyond our control.”
Very poor management and zero care for passengers. I do not recommend flying with Saudia Airlines.

raw_text:
Horrible staff and horrible airline policies Our experience with Saudi airlines was horrible. Airline has horrible policies and their staff is extremely unhelpful and rude.

We had a flight from Toronto to Jeddah with a connection Jeddah to Medina. After that we were gonna take a flight from Makkah to Lahore with a connection in Dubai and lastly Lahore to Toronto. Flight from Toronto was 1 and a half hour late and we ended up missing our connecting flight to Jeddah. We have three kids and just taking them to washrooms after landing meant we barely made to the gate 10 minutes before the plane was supposed to fly. They closed the gates and sent us to one counter to another counter where all the staff said they can’t help and the system has cancelled all of our flights for the rest of the trip. Mind you that’s before the flight even took off. Apparently if we didn’t get on Jeddah flight, system thinks we have missed Toronto flight and won’t be taking rest of the flight which are days and weeks away. I wonder how the system didn’t pick on us getting on Toronto to Jeddah flight????!!!! It was so so disappointing and frustrating … and our frustration and inability to understand the system was frustrating for the staff??? They were passing rude comments and asking us to shut up (like literally a girl at guest service told me to shut up) and were just sending us to one counter to another without giving any proper direction. Airline doesn’t take any responsibility of their passengers travelling for Umarah and have no fear of Allah. Now we are stranded at the airport … trying to figure out what to do next with three kids and no where to go. This is just the Airlines way of making more money … now tickets are 1000s of dollars more expensive and staff is not even willing to find new tickets for us and telling us to work through our agent who has no access to their system and our original tickets anymore. Never in this life we will every travel through Saudi Airlines and I would advise to everyone to not be fooled by occasional price drop … they will make sure you miss one step and they can get you to pay much much more then you would have paid for any better airlines.

raw_text:
I am submitting this complaint as a frequent business class traveler and a long-time customer who has chosen Saudi Airlines based on the trust and high expectations associated with this type of travel. What happened to me at Jeddah Airport while attempting to board an international flight was a genuine shock, not due to any procedural error or glitch, but because of the unprofessional and disrespectful manner in which I was treated.

During my interaction with a supervisor and his colleague, I calmly and clearly explained my experience. Instead of listening or attempting to verify, I was surprised when the supervisor directly told me that everything I was saying was untrue and that what I was describing never happened. This wasn't a difference of opinion; it was a blatant and direct denial of my experience, delivered in a way that implied I was fabricating events or misunderstanding, despite being the affected party and the customer.

What's even more disturbing is that this denial was repeated multiple times, in a defensive and aggressive tone, without any attempt at compromise or even neutrality. I felt like I was being forced to defend my honesty instead of receiving service or a solution. This type of communication is not only unprofessional, but it also amounts to belittling the customer and questioning their integrity—a completely unacceptable behavior, especially from someone in a supervisory position.

As a business class passenger, I expect respectful, attentive, and accountable treatment—not accusations, denial, or blaming the customer for a mistake they didn't make. The gap between these expectations and what actually happened was significant and disheartening.

The impact of this experience wasn't immediate. I felt insulted, unappreciated, and that my long-standing loyalty was worthless at the first sign of trouble. Therefore, calmly and clearly, I decided not to fly with Saudi Arabian Airlines in the future and to switch to Qatar Airways, where difficult experiences are handled professionally and respectfully, not with denial and disbelief.

raw_text:
I arrived at the airport at 3 pm on January 18th. My flight was at 5:30 pm. I had already checked in, but I needed to check my luggage, which I had paid extra for (32 kilos plus an additional 3 kilos). There were many people in line, and it took a long time for me to be served. There were people from other flights in line as well, which delayed things even more. When it was almost 4 pm, the attendant did everything she could to jeopardize my flight, demanding a visa to enter Dubai. I told her I was Brazilian and that Brazilians didn't need a visa to enter Dubai. I also told her I had flown to Dubai before, but she wouldn't listen and told me to talk to one person after another, but nothing was resolved. After a long time, almost an hour later, the gentleman finally attended to me, and I told him my flight was at 5:30 pm and that I couldn't miss it. They simply and blatantly said I would have to buy another ticket because the gates had already been closed, but it wasn't my fault, I was there at the correct time and I have photos and proof that I was there. Anyway, I bought another ticket for the following flight, but I want and demand an apology from the airline, because I fulfilled my obligations as a consumer and it wasn't my mistake if the attendant didn't know how to do their job properly. In short, I paid 359 SAR for the first ticket including excess baggage + the second ticket which cost 400 SAR + 459 for the baggage I bought at the last minute. I have all the receipts and photos showing that I arrived at the correct time.

To compensate for the oncoming flight, which I bought and paid for with my own money, they gave me a box with a horrible snack and terrible apple juice that seemed to have been stale for many days. This is not fair. I've already sent an email and haven't received any response. I've been sending emails since Sunday the 18th, and today is Tuesday the 21st of January, and no one has given me an answer. I've researched this and the company is legally obligated to provide compensation since I didn't miss the flight through any fault of my own.

Schlechter Board Service Also unser Rückflug von DXB nach Amsterdam war über Jeddah Saudi Arabien.
Die Flugzeuge waren schon modern,aber der über beiden Flugzeuge war der Service reine Katastrophe.
Wir haben pro Person jeweils nur eine kleine Flasche à 200 ml Wasser bekommen und das essen war noch nicht besonders.
Beim Essen bekommt man ein kleines Getränk und Tee oder Kaffee .
Wir haben in dem zweiten Flugzeug von Jeddah nach Amsterdam gefragt,warum bekommt man bei eine Flugzeit 6-7 Stunden nur ein 200 ml Wasser.
Am Schluss kurz vor der Landung,nachdem wir gesagt haben,dass wir einen Feedback geben werden.
Dann hat der Service versucht uns Wasser zu geben.
Was soll man mit mit moderne Flugzeuge machen,wenn der Service on Board so eine Katastrophe ist.
Bei Saudi Airlines muss man immer betteln,um etwas Wasser etc.zu bekommen,furchtbar.
Wir werden weder die Saudi Airlines wieder fliegen und noch weiter empfehlen.
Außerdem am Flughafen Jeddah ist so sehr kalt,dass man nachher erkältet ist
Wir haben bis heute eine Erkältung.
translated_text: Poor onboard service Our return flight from DXB to Amsterdam was via Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. The planes were modern, but the service on both flights was a complete disaster. We each received only a small 200 ml bottle of water, and the food was not special. With the meal, you get a small drink and tea or coffee. On the second plane from Jeddah to Amsterdam, we asked why for a 6-7 hour flight you only get 200 ml of water. At the end, just before landing, after we said we would give feedback, the service tried to give us water. What use are modern planes if onboard service is such a disaster? With Saudi Airlines, you always have to beg for water, etc. Terrible. We will neither fly Saudi Airlines again nor recommend them. Also, at Jeddah airport, it is so cold that you end up catching a cold. We still have a cold today.

Formal Complaint Regarding Booking Date Error and Refusal to Assist Formal Complaint Regarding Booking Date Error and Refusal to Assist
I am writing to formally complain about a serious issue I experienced with my flight booking and the lack of support provided by your staff.
I booked my flight online for 7/1/2026, and I have clear proof from the booking website confirming this date. However, when I arrived at the airport, I was informed that my ticket was booked for 6/1/2026, which is incorrect.
Despite presenting valid proof of my online booking showing the correct date (7/1/2026), the airport staff refused to acknowledge the error or assist me in resolving the issue. They remained firm on their position without offering any solution or escalation.
As a result of this mistake:
I was treated as if I had missed my flight
I was forced to purchase a new ticket at my own expense
I arrived late at my destination
I faced a serious crisis at my workplace due to the delay
Additionally, the customer service at the airport was unhelpful. I was repeatedly told to call a customer service number, and I spent the entire day trying to reach someone for assistance. Unfortunately, no one helped resolve the issue, either at the airport or via customer support.
I later filed an official complaint requesting a refund for the additional ticket and compensation, as the issue was clearly caused by a system or booking error. However, my request was refused without a fair investigation, despite the evidence I provided.

raw_text:
Airline failed to honor my confirmed and issued ticket I booked a flight and selected my seat during booking. After payment, my ticket was confirmed and issued.
I received this confirmation email with the ticket number, seat specified all well 3 months in advance. Also received a reminder from the airline itself 1 week before departure.

Despite this, when I went to check in, I was told that my seat had been given to someone else and that my reservation could not be found. I had to show my receipt for the additional service (seat), ticket confirmation email to prove that I had a valid, paid, and issued ticket.

What happened next was honestly shocking. The case was escalated to a supervisor, who told me, “if you had come earlier, this wouldn’t have happened,” and ask me to decide whether I wanted to fly or not, even though I was already there well in advance and the delay was caused internally.

The supervisor also said it was not their responsibility because they did not personally make the change and done by someone else internally.

I could not believe what I heard and also being blamed for something I did not cause felt extremely unfair.

Once a ticket is issued, the airline is responsible for honoring it and they clearly failed.

A trip I had carefully planned in advance quickly turned into a nightmare.

SkyTeam Elite Plus and Saudia Gold status also made no difference. I would not recommend investing time in loyalty with an airline that can break trust this easily for its own convenience.

For a long-haul flight, this was a very stressful and disappointing experience. I raised complaint and still havent got the refund.

Unreliable, unprofessional. I want to express my extreme dissatisfaction with the service I received during my recent trip. My flight from Washington to Riyadh was delayed by more than two hours due to maintenance issues, causing me to miss my connecting flight to Dubai. I was rebooked on a flight six hours later.

Upon arrival in Riyadh, the transit service was extremely unprofessional. Staff members raised their voices at me, offered no apology, and refused my request to speak with a manager to have an earlier connection. Additionally, the call support center remained unreachable. Despite the long delay caused by the airline, no food vouchers, lounge access, or any form of accommodation or assistance were provided. Furthermore, your website states that if a traveler’s arrival is delayed by more than three hours, they are entitled to compensation. Despite meeting this criterion, I was not offered any compensation or guidance on how to claim it.

This experience was unacceptable, and based on this treatment, I am unwilling to use this airline again.

raw_text:
I flew with Flynas and I have to say, unfortunately, that this was by far the worst airline experience I've ever had. From check-in to boarding, from the staff to the organization – there were problems, rudeness, and complete indifference towards passengers at every stage.

The trouble started on the outbound flight. I had purchased 20 kilograms of checked baggage, as clearly stated in the booking. I had two suitcases with me, one weighing 12 kilograms and the other 8 kilograms – exactly 20 kilograms in total.

Nevertheless, I was told at the counter that I could only check in one suitcase. The weight supposedly didn't matter. The rule wasn't explained, there was no logical justification, and absolutely no willingness to find a solution. The staff were unfriendly, seemed annoyed, and clearly had no interest in even addressing the problem.

But the return journey was even worse. We were a group of eight people who wanted to travel together. The manager on site explicitly told us to use the express lane to speed up check-in. He also passed this information on to the staff at the express lane.

As soon as we arrived, an employee suddenly told us we all had to come in individually. Immediately afterward, the same employee simply sent us out of the queue. We had to leave the express lane and rejoin the regular, much longer line.

The whole thing was not only unprofessional but completely disrespectful, especially for a group of several people with luggage.

I tried to speak calmly and objectively with the responsible officer or the manager at the counter. But there was absolutely no willingness to listen. No response, no explanation, no attempt to accommodate me. The staff turned away from each other and took no responsibility whatsoever. It's incomprehensible to me that an airline has employees who behave like this.

The entire process was chaotic and disorganized. You got the feeling that everyone was doing whatever they wanted and that nobody actually knew what the rules were. Everyone says something different, every counter has its own version of the "rules," and in the end, you're simply sent away, regardless of whether you're in the right or not.

The atmosphere on the plane itself was tense. The flight attendants were unfriendly, barely communicated, and seemed annoyed with every passenger. There was practically no help or service. Even simple questions were answered coldly.

For all these reasons, I can say quite clearly:

I will never fly flynas again.

The airline is disorganized, unfriendly, and customer-unfriendly.

There are many other airlines that operate professionally and treat their passengers with respect. Flynas is definitely not one of them, in my opinion.

My first experience flying with this airline was extremely disappointing. The check-in staff treated us disrespectfully, and I was shocked to be charged 30 SAR just to print a boarding pass—especially after multiple failed attempts to check in online due to issues with their system.

What made things worse was the unacceptable treatment of my mother, who is wheelchair-bound. The staff refused to allow her wheelchair on the aircraft with us and insisted it must be shipped separately unless we paid 180 SAR. They even required us to wrap it for an additional 50 SAR. I have never seen any airline treat a disabled passenger this way.

All these unexpected charges and the long arguments almost made us miss our flight. After traveling all the way from Amsterdam to spend the summer in Saudi Arabia, this ended up being one of the worst travel experiences I have ever had.

raw_text: SV820 Saudi Airlines bad luck December 9, 2025, at 1:30 PM, we were at the departure gate with our boarding passes in hand, waiting for 12 hours without any certainty. Only a piece of bread , kitkat, juice without drinking water as compensation, there was no prior notification that there would be a delay of more than 20 hours. Passengers from the waiting room were packed onto buses to the plane, waiting for 40 minutes on the bus until some elderly passengers had difficulty breathing because they were so crowded, and finally the bus was returned to the waiting room again. After 12 hours, we were informed that there was a delay of 20 hours and 30 minutes, then passengers were directed to the nearest hotel , thank for breakfast. 4 hours later, we were escorted back to the departure gate. I never thought that Saudi Airlines' service with its "Skyteam" would be so embarrassing.

raw_text:
Lost Baggage! Dear Saudia Airlines Customer Service,

I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding a serious issue that occurred during my recent journey with Saudia Airlines.

During check-in, one of my hand-luggage items was taken from me to be placed in the aircraft hold. However, no baggage tag was issued or attached to this item, despite my asking for confirmation. This is a significant breach of proper check-in procedures and has directly contributed to what happened next.

Following the flight, the bag was not delivered and has been missing ever since, with no updates provided. I have repeatedly attempted to obtain information about its status, but the lack of response and clarity has been unacceptable.

This situation has caused considerable stress and inconvenience, especially because the bag contains important personal belongings. Given that the bag was taken from me at the check-in counter without a tag, I expect Saudia Airlines to treat this matter with urgency and conduct a thorough investigation.

raw_text:
Travel Chaos with Saudia Airline. My family and I travelled to Jeddah, the city where I grew up, on the 19th December. What should have been a meaningful and joyful trip turned into one of the most frustrating travel experiences of my life. We arrived at the airport in Riyadh by 7 a.m. for our 9 a.m. flight. Yet when we checked the departure boards, our flight wasn’t even listed. We waited — confused and anxious — for over an hour before discovering from the screen, not from staff, that our flight had been delayed. Then delayed again. And again. 11 a.m. became 12 p.m., then 1 p.m., with no explanation, no announcements, and no accountability. Eventually, a gate number appeared. There, we met dozens of other stranded passengers — many of whom had been waiting since 1 a.m., most since 4 a.m. — all equally uninformed. The few staff members we managed to find were completely unhelpful, offering nothing but vague responses and empty stares. No clarity. No honesty. No leadership. What made the situation even more infuriating was watching other airlines, including Flynas, operate normally while not a single Saudia flight departed. After more than 12 exhausting hours, we were finally informed — not by staff, but by an app — that our flight was cancelled. Collecting our luggage took yet another painfully long wait. When we finally reached customer service, we were met with a massive queue and, effectively, silence. I was initially denied the chance to speak to anyone at all. When I finally did, I asked one simple question: Why was the flight delayed? The response was cowardly and insulting: “Natural causes — therefore we are not liable for compensation.”
What makes this even worse is that Saudia’s own Customer Rights Policy states: 1 hour delay → refreshments 2 hours → a meal 6 hours → 150% ticket refund, rebooking, and hotel accommodation, etc. We received none of this - not even a glass of water. Instead, we were told to “go home and wait.” This experience revealed an airline built on empty promises, deflection, and dishonesty. The lack of transparency, care, and basic responsibility was shocking.

raw_text: "Hiding behind fare policies" Due to a name mismatch (initials instead of full name), I was denied boarding at the airport, despite offering valid identification and requesting alternative verification. No solution was provided on the spot. I was travelling for Umrah with my husband and young son. Because of this issue, I was forced to travel alone, leaving my family behind. I even tried to rebook on the same flight through Marhaba at Dubai Terminal 1, but the booking got cancelled. Before making alternate arrangements, I asked Flynas staff whether my return ticket would also be cancelled, and I was told it would be auto-cancelled since the outbound flight was not travelled. My visa was a single-entry Umrah visa, leaving me with no flexibility. I had no choice but to book another airline at the last minute, which cost me around AED 3,000 extra. What made this worse was the customer service after the incident: Emails received only generic copy-paste policy replies. Call center attempts did not go through even after waiting a long time. Hiding behind “non-refundable fare” policies is unacceptable.

raw_text:
Failure and Mishandling of Checked-In Luggage – Flyadeal Flight F3 651 (RUH–ISB) I would like to share a worst experience with Flyadeal airline.

Complaint is regarding the unacceptable handling of checked-in luggage on Flight F3 651 from Riyadh (RUH) to Islamabad (ISB) on 25 Dec 2025, scheduled to depart at 22:20 and arrive at 04:20.

Upon arrival at Islamabad Int. Airport, it was discovered that none of the checked-in luggage for the passengers on this flight arrived with the aircraft.

After repeated inquiries with the airport authorities in Islamabad, they said, Flyadeal is entirely responsible for this failure, and that all passenger luggage was left behind in Riyadh.

We were further informed that the luggage would only be transported on a next flight from Riyadh to Islamabad, expected after two days, which is completely unacceptable. This situation caused significant inconvenience, stress, and hardship, particularly for passengers who were traveling with essential personal belongings, clothing, medicines, and gifts.

Such a lapse reflects poor operational planning, lack of accountability, and a complete disregard for passenger convenience and basic service standards. Leaving all passengers without their luggage upon international arrival is a serious service failure and falls far below acceptable aviation and customer service practices.

This experience has caused severe disruption to travel plans and has left an extremely negative impression of Flyadeal’s reliability and professionalism. I strongly urge your team to:

- Provide a clear written explanation for this incident 
  
- Confirm the exact status and delivery timeline of the delayed luggage 
  
- Explain what compensation will be offered to affected passengers for the inconvenience caused 

I expect this complaint to be treated with urgency and seriousness. Failure to address this matter appropriately will leave me with no option but to escalate it to the relevant aviation and consumer protection authorities.

Sincerely, 
Zakka Khan 
Passenger – Flight F3 651 
Riyadh to Islamabad 
25 December 2025

raw_text:
Saudi airline is the worst in the world On the 29th of October we were going to pakistan landing in lahore, but unfortunately we missed the flight as my daughter who is 5 years old had dihorria and we were constantly going to the toilet. My son who was 6 months old at the time also needed a nappy change at the time. We didnt realise that gate had closed cause usually they wait for passengers to come even after announcing the final boarding announcement. Our flight was due to take off at 5:20 and we got to the gate at 5:05. The aeroplane had not departed just yet and was still stood outside but took off after 5 minutes. Im not sure why the aeroplane took off at 5:10 when it was due to take off at 5:20, considering we had children and given our circumstances the staff could have allowed us to board as we still had time to departure. After that we were sent to the customer information desk, they asked us for the boarding passes and 4 out of 5 boarding passes did not mention that the gate closes 20 mins before departure. It was only written on my sons hamza saqibs boarding pass which we unfortunately did not see. We pressurised the staff to find us next available flights as i had a little baby and it was not possible for us to stay at the airport as we had limited formula and nappies and clothes. But they said that there's no flights available so we have to find our own. Eventually we managed to find a flight after a few hours. We asked the staff to give us our luggage, they said that the luggage is in the basement and it doesn't go on the flight if the passengers miss their flight but he ensured us that our luggage will be on the flight that we choose to go on. During the night the staff had changed and another team member took over the other staff member, when we told him we found another flight and mentioned our luggage he said that he could not promise that our luggage will go on the flight as we gave short notice and usually they need 2- 4 hours notice. I stated that I have a small baby who is 6 months. His essentials were in the suitcases so it was important that the luggage comes with us. He said that he try his best but cannot promise that all the luggage will board with us.
My wife and children then got on the flight that we had booked, there was no space for me so I had to wait 24 hours to get on the next available flight. The flight which we was able to get was taking us to the Islamabad airport in pakistan so when my wife and children landed they went to collect the luggage they waited an hour to get the luggage but the luggage never came. My wife then reported it to the missing baggage department. They said to my wife that our luggage is most likely at the lahore airport but to confirm this they took our details and phone number and stated that they will give us a call. In the afternoon we get a call saying that it is at the lahore airport. We was not happy about this because the staff at jeddah airport had lied to us that our luggage is at jeddah and has not boarded even though we asked them so many times to confirm and they still kept lying even though they knew we had little children. This was really unprofessional of them and we were not happy about this. We had to wait 2 days to get our luggage considering we have a baby. These people don't care about anyone they need some qualified staff and soo rude they are.
Bycott saudi airline

raw_text:
The worst service I've ever experienced in my 15 years of travel.

My experience with them was a transit flight from Dubai to Riyadh and then from Riyadh to Cairo on December 3, 2025. I missed my connecting flight from Riyadh to Cairo due to the short time between flights. I booked a flight with Saudi Airlines to Cairo because there were no flights until December 7. Upon arrival in Cairo, my luggage didn't arrive. I filed a lost report and contacted them almost daily, but to no avail. My luggage still hasn't arrived, and I haven't received any compensation. My return flight was also with them on December 29, 2025, but the flight supervisor didn't help me at all. He told me my luggage was outside passport control at the FlyNas office, and since I don't have a visa for Saudi Arabia, I can't leave. All I have to do is repeat what happened before I arrived at my destination: file another report, and my luggage will arrive on the next flight. But it hasn't. I'm still in contact, but to no avail. May God suffice me, and He is the best disposer of affairs. My luggage contains items worth approximately 20,000 dirhams. Please note that my flight number (XY269) is from Dubai to Riyadh, and this is my baggage number: XY059357418

raw_text: Estafa Stopover Visa Atención al cliente pésima. Antes de empezar el viaje, intenté sacar el stopover visa para poder visitar Riad, y al llegar al método de pago, el sistema no lo tramitaba. Después de intercambiar numerosas llamadas y correos, empecé el viaje sin tener una visa para Riad. Cuando llegué a Riad, me obligaron a pagar la Visa On Arrival, ya que al hacer escala pasaba de una terminal internacional a una local. Ahí hablé con el director que había en el mostrador de la aerolínea y me dijo que reclamara de forma online. Puse la reclamación con muchas dificultades, ya que esa parte de la web también dejaba de funcionar durante el trámite, y todavía sigo esperando una respuesta de la compañía. Si queréis volar con ellos y hacer un stopover, tened en cuenta que acabaréis pagando 100€ más por la visa y no os lo devolverán, aunque prometan en su web una tarifa reducida.
translated_text: Scam Stopover Visa Poor customer service. Before starting the trip, I tried to get the stopover visa to be able to visit Riyadh, and when I reached the payment method, the system did not process it. After exchanging numerous calls and emails, I started the trip without having a visa for Riyadh. When I arrived in Riyadh, I was forced to pay the Visa On Arrival, since when making a stopover I was moving from an international terminal to a local one. There I spoke with the manager at the airline counter and he told me to file a claim online. I filed the claim with great difficulty, as that part of the website also stopped working during the process, and I am still waiting for a response from the company. If you want to fly with them and make a stopover, keep in mind that you will end up paying 100€ more for the visa and they will not refund it, even though they promise a reduced rate on their website.

raw_text:
None stop noise I’m leaving two stars because the flight attendants were professional and courteous, and the food and beverage service was excellent. However, the flight itself was very uncomfortable due to excessive noise from several families with young children. The situation was not well managed, and the constant crying and shouting made it difficult to rest or enjoy the long-haul journey.
Additionally, the condition of one of the lavatories was unacceptable, as some passengers did not maintain proper hygiene standards. This seems to occur more frequently on certain long routes where large groups travel together, and I encourage the airline to enforce better cleanliness and cabin etiquette.
While I appreciate the kindness of the crew, the overall experience was far from relaxing, and I would not be flying with this airline again.

raw_text:
Sicher und komfortabel ans Ziel Alle Flüge waren pünktlich. Die gereichten Speisen an Bord waren ausreichend und von sehr guter Qualität. Bordpersonal freundlich, kundenorentiert und sehr gepflegt.
Fühlte mich jederzeit sicher und gut betreut an Bord. Es gibt keine alkoholischen Getränke an Bord, dafür leckere Säfte. Umsteigen in Djeddah problemlos und schnell, sehr angenehmer 'ruhiger' Flughafen mit zahlreichen Sitzmöglichkeiten. Sehr gutes Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis.
Achtung: Der Flex Tarif erlaubt zwar die Umbuchung, im Falle einer Umbuchung gehen aber sämtliche Sitzplatzreservierungen verloren, auch für die nicht umgebuchte Strecke.
Sitzplatzreservierungen für alle Strecken müssen erneut gebucht und noch einmal bezahlt werden!
Schade dass man auf keinem der Flüge eine Begrüssung aus dem Cockpit gehört hat.
translated_text: Safe and comfortable to the destination All flights were on time. The meals served on board were sufficient and of very good quality. Cabin crew friendly, customer-oriented, and very well-groomed. I always felt safe and well cared for on board. There are no alcoholic beverages on board, but delicious juices instead. Transfers in Jeddah were easy and quick, a very pleasant 'quiet' airport with numerous seating options. Very good value for money. Note: The Flex fare allows rebooking, but in case of rebooking, all seat reservations are lost, even for the non-rebooked segment. Seat reservations for all segments must be booked again and paid for again! It's a pity that on none of the flights was there a greeting from the cockpit.

raw_text:
Deeply Disappointed by Disrespectful Behavior from Cabin Crew Member “Ahmad” – Flyadeal F3829 (Jeddah → Karachi, 30 Nov I am writing this as the son of a 63-year-old passenger who experienced extremely rude and unacceptable behavior from one of Flyadeal’s cabin crew members, “Ahmad,” on Flight F3829 from Jeddah to Karachi on 30th November 2025 (1:05 AM departure).

My father, who has genuine medical issues, was carrying a slightly heavy hand-carry bag and needed assistance placing it into the overhead compartment. He politely asked the crew member, Ahmad, for help. Instead of offering basic courtesy—let alone compassion for an elderly passenger—Ahmad bluntly replied:

“It’s not my job.”

My father calmly explained that he had a medical condition and genuinely could not lift the bag. Ahmad’s response was shockingly heartless:

“That’s not my problem.”

When my father, feeling humiliated, said he would file a complaint, Ahmad dismissed him again, saying:

“Do whatever you want and sit down on your seat.”

As his son, watching my father—an elderly man who rarely asks anyone for help—be treated with such disrespect and arrogance was absolutely heartbreaking. No passenger should ever be spoken to like this, especially someone older with medical limitations. Cabin crew are trained to ensure safety and basic human decency onboard, and this behavior was the opposite of everything Flyadeal claims to stand for.

We are frequent travelers and have never encountered such dismissive, unprofessional conduct from any airline staff. This experience left my father upset and embarrassed and left our entire family extremely disappointed in Flyadeal’s service standards.

I sincerely hope the airline reviews this incident seriously, retrains or disciplines the staff member involved, and ensures no other elderly or medically challenged passenger is treated this way again.

Respect and empathy cost nothing—but to vulnerable passengers, they mean everything.

Urgent Help Needed! Lost Luggage on JED-KHI Flight F3829 (29-Nov-2025) I am deeply frustrated and disappointed with my first-time travel experience with Flyadeal.
On 29-Nov-2025, I traveled from Jeddah to Karachi on Flight F3829, but my luggage—containing important personal items—never arrived. I immediately reported the missing bag to Menzies-RAS/Flyadeal at Karachi Airport and filed a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) ,Tag Number F3319737., File Reference number KHIF310289 PNR D933UW
Since then, despite:
• Repeated visits to the airport and follow-ups with Menzies-RAS
• Multiple calls to the FLYADEAL airline helpline
• Submitting online complaint forms
there has been no response, no guidance, and no information about my bag. No one is answering calls, replying to emails, or providing any clarity on when I can expect my belongings.
As a passenger, I feel completely ignored and helpless.
please escalate this urgently and help me recover my luggage.

Waiting for your urgent reply. Regards

raw_text: A terrible experience, and I don't recommend using Flynas. I traveled with them from Abha to Riyadh, and on the return flight to Abha, I was stopped by a company employee at the boarding gate. He asked me for my boarding sticker after looking at my ticket, which I hadn't obtained from the check-in counter. He then said, "Sorry, you're not allowed to board. This mistake isn't my responsibility." Also, how did I get to the boarding gate? Where were the airport and airline staff if anyone can reach the plane door without being questioned? I didn't get on the plane, and the employee refused to check my ticket, which is a necessary procedure to verify the ticket information. The company didn't compensate me, and there's no customer service whatsoever. I apologize for the long post, but I wanted to share my experience so that no one else loses the price of a ticket due to an employee's negligence. Thank you.

I want to share my very disappointing experience with Saudia Airlines.

I am a person with a disability and I received absolutely no assistance. I had to wait for over three hours sitting on very uncomfortable chairs, surrounded by bulky suitcases, which made moving around extremely difficult.

Every time I asked for assistance, I was simply told to wait. No one came. As a result, I didn't get through security or passport control until the very last minute. The organization was so disorganized that I almost missed my flight.

No support, no priority, no consideration for a person with a disability. This is unacceptable for an airline.

I strongly advise against flying with this airline. For a stress-free journey, it's better to choose Emirates or Qatar Airways and avoid Saudia Airlines.

raw_text: The staff I was seeing at the check-in counter in Jeddah yesterday at 7 PM, during Maghrib prayer time, were among the worst I've ever encountered. Their behavior was utterly disrespectful, with no consideration or consideration whatsoever. One of them even raised her voice at the employee with her; her attitude was incredibly vulgar. The flight attendants were also completely unfit for their jobs, frankly. Similarly, when I disembarked from my flight from Riyadh on February 23rd, around 1 PM, I requested a wheelchair from the receptionist in Jeddah because I'm unable to walk. She refused, telling me not to wait and to walk a bit to find the exit, even though I had already explained that I didn't need the exit. I was shocked to find that the exit was very far away, until I reached the parking lot where I had booked a car to pick me up from the airport and take me to Mecca. In short, I swear I will never use this airline again. I give them a zero.

raw_text:
Saudi Airlines' international flights generally offer very good service, but unfortunately, my return flight from Casablanca, Morocco to Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, was not up to par. The outbound flight was much better, with a more luxurious aircraft and all the necessary amenities.

My return flight, SV 372, was far from satisfactory, especially due to the flight attendant's aggressive and inappropriate manner. She treated most passengers harshly. When I asked her to change seats, she refused and immediately responded with, "What do you want?"

Even though I spoke to another flight attendant, who said she would wait until everyone had finished. Her aggressive attitude is unacceptable in a workplace. Unprofessional staff should not be employed. In fact, all the other flight attendants were very professional and courteous, except for this one.

This was in addition to the overcrowding and constant interruptions.

I urge the relevant authorities to look into this matter. Thank you.

raw_text:
Extremely Poor Customer Service and Unfair Handling by Flynas Unfortunately, my experience with Flynas was one of the worst travel experiences I have ever had, and I will never fly with them again.

My flight from Dammam to Jeddah on 21 / … at 9:30 AM was cancelled by the airline. As a result, I was forced to rebook another flight at 1:55 AM, arriving in Jeddah at 4:20 AM instead of the originally scheduled 12:35 PM arrival.

This cancellation caused significant inconvenience and financial loss, including:
• Having to check into the hotel very early and pay for an extra night.
• Cancelling my original train booking and purchasing a new ticket due to the change in arrival time.
• A complete disruption to my travel plans, which were all based on the original flight schedule.

What made the situation even worse was the extremely poor customer service. When I contacted Flynas via WhatsApp, the customer service agent ended the conversation abruptly while I was explaining the issue and provided no real assistance. Emails were ignored, and it took four phone calls before someone finally answered.

Despite the cancellation being entirely Flynas’ responsibility, I was told that I was at fault and was asked to pay fees to change my return flight, which is completely unreasonable.

A professional airline should take responsibility for its own cancellations and support its passengers properly. Instead, I experienced unhelpful staff, long response times, and a lack of accountability.

Rating: 1/5
I do not recommend Flynas and will not be flying with them again.

raw_text:
What a terrible experience. We arrived three hours before our flight. The check-in staff was incredibly slow, resulting in a long queue. When it was our turn to check in at counter C19, the staff member looked confused. She then left us for 30 minutes. Then she returned and told us to check in at counter C2. After we queued again at counter C2, a male staff member scanned our passports and said our details weren't on the passenger list. After waiting for a while, he then told us to leave because the boarding time had passed. We missed our flight because of these two slow staff members!
Finally, we tried to resolve the issue with Saudia Airlines Guestcare. The unfriendly female staff member casually checked our details and said they were on the passenger list. So why did the check-in staff member turn us away and say our details weren't there?
Because of this incident, we were deemed a "no-show" for our missed flight.

raw_text:
Complaint regarding flight SV1547 on 2nd December, 2025 To the Management of Saudi Airlines,

I am writing to express my deep disappointment and frustration regarding my recent experience with Saudi Airlines on flight SV1547, reservation number 9AN6SQ, on December 2, 2025.

I had a confirmed and paid reservation from Jizan to Tabuk, with a scheduled connection in Jeddah. Upon arriving in Jeddah for my onward flight, I was shocked to be informed at the check-in counter that my seat had been given to another passenger. The ground staff stated that my reservation had been intentionally assigned to someone else.

The proposed solution was unacceptable. I was told to wait until all other passengers had boarded, and only if a seat became available due to a cancellation would I be allowed to fly. If not, I would be provided a hotel until the next day's flight. This caused me significant distress, inconvenience, and a profound sense of disrespect as a paying customer.

This situation raises serious concerns:

1. How and why was my confirmed and paid reservation deliberately given to another passenger?
2. I learned that two other passengers on the same flight faced the identical issue. This indicates a systemic failure or a serious breach of procedure, not an isolated error.

I demand a thorough and immediate investigation into this incident. I require a detailed written explanation of:

· The exact cause of this failure.
· The steps being taken to ensure it never happens again.
· Appropriate compensation for the complete disruption to my travel plans, the significant distress caused, and the wasted time and potential costs incurred.

My confidence in Saudi Airlines has been severely damaged by this incident. I expect your prompt and serious attention to this matter. Please provide your response and resolution proposal via email at your earliest convenience.

Mohsin,

“Faced seat mismanagement and rude, threatening crew behavior on Saudia Airline SV734 when asking for my rightful seat. I usually don’t post complaints publicly, but my recent experience on Saudia Arline SV734 dated 06 Nov 2025 was deeply concerning and needs attention.
From the moment I boarded, there was complete mismanagement of seating. My assigned seat was occupied, and despite standing for a long time, no crew member assisted. I was later moved to a temporary seat right in front of the washroom—uncomfortable, noisy, and unpleasant. I accepted it only to help the flight depart on time.
After takeoff, when I politely requested to return to my original seat due to discomfort, a staff member shockingly responded with yelling, threats, and aggressive behavior. I was even told security would be called—simply for asking for the seat printed on my boarding pass.
When I requested the supervisor, the situation became even worse. He was dismissive, rude, and repeatedly said he “couldn’t do anything.” At one point, he even told me to “do whatever you want.” Only after I firmly insisted did he finally check my boarding pass and return me to my rightful seat.
This was not just poor service—it was unprofessional, intimidating, and completely unacceptable from a national airline that represents the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
I am sharing this not for sympathy, but to highlight the urgent need for accountability, staff training, and respect for passengers. I hope Saudia takes this seriously and ensures no one else goes through such treatment.

Dear Flyadeal Team,

I am extremely disappointed with my recent experience with Flyadeal. I had booked flights from Riyadh to Jeddah and back, but your system and mobile application were very poor. The app was not working properly, and I was unable to complete my online check-in.

When I went to the counter to check in, I was charged SAR 120 for my family’s check-in, which I find completely unfair. Your check-in service is extremely poor and needs serious improvement. Online check-in should be a free and convenient option for passengers, but instead, it caused me frustration and extra cost.

I expected better service from Flyadeal, but this experience has left me very disappointed. I hope you take this feedback seriously and improve your system and customer service.

raw_text:
Cheap, budget standard airline. I would look at the alternatives before using this airline again.

The Good: it’s cheap and I got to and from my destination.

The Bad: Why didn’t the airline tell me that in Pakistan, checking in online means nothing? At passport control, after queuing for half an hour, I was sent back to the Check-in desk to collect physical boarding cards!!

We were called to board in both directions, then had to stand in a queue for 15 minutes? Why? Why not call people to board when you are ready to board? Yesterday, when we did get to ‘board’ we were then held at the plane entrance a further 10 minutes. The old man at the front could clearly not walk well and was propped up by his aging wife - was it really so hard for Saudi staff to spot the issue and to get the man a seat? Maybe if they weren’t on their mobiles 90% of the time?

Riyadh has to be one of the worst airports to have a long stopover in. Ridiculously bright, seats designed to stop you getting comfy and gave a nap, nowhere to get your head down, and one out of hundreds of charging points that worked - seriously? Nearly every one was broken, had the fuse missing or were disabled. I found one socket working, in a corner in the upstairs food hall. This is appalling management.

The in-flight food was inedible. It really was poor stodgy rubbish.

My return flight had many free seats. Yet I was jammed in to where there were lots of very noisy young children. The attendants could see there was a problem and could gave at least offered to move some of us desperate to sleep.

My biggest complaint are the seats. They are seriously uncomfortable, using slippery fake leather with little legroom and a poor recline. Sleeping was all but impossible. I was totally exhausted and exasperated by the time I got off the flight.
