Unreliable Baggage Handling and Unhelpful Customer Service Experience I am very disappointed with the level of customer service I received from Saudia Airlines.

My baggage was offloaded without my knowledge, even though a customer service agent clearly told me that it would arrive at my destination. When I discovered this, I tried to file a baggage claim, but the online claim form does not work properly — it keeps loading endlessly. I also contacted customer service several times, but no one was able to provide real assistance or a clear solution.

It’s unacceptable that such a major airline has such poor communication and unreliable support, especially when passengers are already dealing with stressful travel issues. Saudia Airlines needs to improve its customer service systems, ensure accurate information is given to passengers, and provide timely help when things go wrong.

I truly hope this feedback is taken seriously and leads to better service for future travelers.

raw_text: Criminal, despicable airline Criminal, corrupt airline. Layers and layers of egregiousness with this horrible company. Cancelled our flights back to the US from Cairo twice, with the second cancellation accompanying a rebooking for nearly TWO WEEKS later, with no alternative flight options. Effectively stranded us in the Mideast with two young children. To make matters worse, the airline provides absolutely no way to contact them while overseas, despite assistance from our Cairo hotel staff, etc. We spent hours searching for a phone number, email address, Whatsapp number, anything, to no avail. Finally, we got a family member in the US to contact them on our behalf, and after multiple phone calls and being given inconsistent information, our fam member was told that the airline would not rebook us on any earlier flights (though they existed both through this airline and sister airlines), and that they could only cancel and offer other options if we paid out of pocket. As if the shock of this approach wasn't enough, when we decided we had no choice but to opt for the cancellation option and rebook ourselves, Saudia Air initially said we could cancel directly with them over the phone for the refund, but when our fam member attempted to do that, they pivoted and said we had to go through Expedia, because the flights had been booked through them initially. Desperate for a resolution, we ultimately paid out of pocket to get ourselves out of Egypt through a different airline, and now, nearly two weeks later, both we and Expedia are still battling this despicable airline for the refund of our cancelled flights and they are being completely unresponsive on all fronts. We are in the process of taking legal action against this airline, filing complaints with the DOT, and have also involved our credit card company. As an addon, if we had waited in Egypt until their proposed date of nearly two weeks after our scheduled flight out, we'd have now been stuck there given the current Mideast conflict, which began after we left the country. Do yourself a favor and avoid Saudia Air like the plague. Their actions essentially amount to criminality/theft in the US.

Vicious Circle or DREADFUL experience with "Flynas" Customer Service online, via email, on WhatsApp and on the phone Vicious Circle, DREADFUL EXPERIENCE with "Flynas" company's CUSTOMER SERVICE (the company has no concern for its customers outside of Jeddah or Riyadh)
March 2026
My review is going to be a VERY NEGATIVE REVIEW for "Flynas" CUSTOMER SERVICE though I am an understanding and considerate person aware of the issues around. If I could give less points, I would have!
"Flynas"'s MAIN target seems to be a manipulation of the customers to gain the maximum profit of moderate income passengers. They skillfully ignored my messages, emails, phone calls , WhatsApp messaging to get a profit of 1848 Riyals without providing ANY SERVICE, and I still have not received the rest of 160 Riyals. I could not by any means to connect to the CS NO MATTER HOW HARD I TRIED FOR 4 DAYS to modify my ticket in order not to lose my hard-earned expensive ticket money (which they call Light Flex.). Alongside that, I contacted Flynas website's Ticket Return online, and I received a message" contact customer service" It was a VICIOUS CIRCLE to make me lose my ticket money!
They are selling their tickets all over Saudi Arabia, and have offices only in Riyadh and Jeddah, and the management cares less about what happens with its customers, as their CS is NON RESPONSIVE especially for English speakers. It was an extremely stressful and anxious experience for me and I advise everybody to STAY AWAY from this airlines which calls itself "the leading low cost airline" in the Middle east having 0 CUSTOMER SERVICE for customers who live outside of Riyadh and Jeddah. Also, they expropriated the luggage from me on the previous flight. First their Airport Control claimed I should pay 200 Riyals for the overweight luggage, and when I approached the check-in counter, the amount went up to 750 Riyals which I did not have with me at that point. After that they claimed they provide my 14 kg full bag of goodies on return, but when I messaged, called and then visited the local airport police department locker where they should have kept the luggage, they did not have my beautiful brown bag, and I lost all my stuff and my textbooks needed for my work. The representative who ignored my messages and calls and requests of help, A.N . works for "Saudia", and who was overlooking "Flynas" at that moment. Also, the "Flynas" airport general manager M.G. gave false promises being polite but not providing ANY assistance in any matter.
Flynas is really a manipulative, promiscuous and ONLY profit-oriented private company which is targeted for income ONLY. STAY AWAY PLEASE
FYI "Saudia" is much better.

extremely disappointed I am  with Flynas and their customer service.

I had a confirmed booking from Amman to Jeddah (flight XY554) scheduled to depart at 22:35. Without any proper solution or consultation, the airline rescheduled my flight to 16:00, which is more than 6 hours earlier and completely disrupts my travel plans.

I immediately contacted Flynas customer service to find a solution—either a flight closer to my original time or proper accommodation if they move me to the next day. Unfortunately, no one has responded, and there is no clear support from the airline despite this being a change initiated by them.

Changing a passenger’s schedule so drastically and then failing to respond to customer inquiries is unacceptable. Travelers rely on airlines to respect their time and provide support when changes happen.

I sincerely hope Flynas resolves this issue quickly, because the current level of customer service is extremely disappointing and far below acceptable standards.

raw_text:
My experience with Saudi Airlines (Saudia) was honestly one of the worst travel experiences I’ve ever had. From the moment I arrived at the airport until I finally got my luggage, the entire journey was frustrating, stressful, and poorly managed.

The problems started at check-in. The lines were extremely long and moved painfully slowly, yet the staff seemed completely uninterested in helping passengers move through the process efficiently. When I finally reached the counter, the staff member was rude and impatient, barely acknowledging basic questions. It felt like customer service simply didn’t exist.

Boarding was another disaster. There was almost no organization at the gate. Passengers were crowding around because announcements were unclear and staff did little to control the situation. It felt chaotic and unprofessional compared to other airlines that manage boarding much more smoothly.

Once onboard, the situation didn’t improve. The cabin crew came across as cold and uninterested. Simple requests such as asking for water or assistance were met with short responses and visible annoyance. Instead of feeling welcomed as a passenger, it felt like the crew saw us as an inconvenience.

The aircraft itself felt outdated and poorly maintained. The seats were uncomfortable, with very little legroom and limited recline. My entertainment screen barely worked, constantly freezing and becoming unresponsive, which made the long flight even more tedious. For an international airline, the condition of the cabin was disappointing.

The food was another low point. The meal was bland, served lukewarm, and lacked quality. The portion size was small and there were hardly any options. It felt like very little effort was put into providing passengers with a decent in-flight meal.

To make things worse, the flight was delayed and there was almost no clear communication from the airline about what was happening. Passengers were left waiting without proper updates. After landing, the baggage claim process was extremely slow, and many passengers, including myself, had to wait a long time for their luggage.

Overall, this experience was far below the standard I expect from an international airline. Poor service, disorganized boarding, uncomfortable seating, bad food, delays, and lack of communication made the entire trip unpleasant. Unfortunately, this flight left a very negative impression, and I would seriously think twice before flying with Saudi Airlines again.

I am writing this to warn fellow travelers about Flynas’s unprofessionalism. I had to cancel my domestic flight due to the Middle East war (Force Majeure), which made it impossible for me to enter Saudi Arabia as my international flight was diverted.
Here are the facts of their failure:

1. Involuntary Cancellation Ignored: Despite the international crisis (war), Flynas refuses to process a full refund. This is an involuntary cancellation beyond my control, yet they are holding my money hostage.

2. Worst Customer Service (Ghosting): I have sent multiple emails and complaints with all the necessary documents over the past 4 days. They have COMPLETELY IGNORED all my inquiries. Zero response, zero accountability.

3. Unfair Refund Policy: They only offer a small amount of "credit" after deducting massive penalties for a situation caused by a war.

Avoid Flynas at all costs if you value your money and peace of mind. They are incapable of handling emergencies and have no respect for passenger rights. I am now proceeding with a bank chargeback and a formal complaint to GACA.

raw_text:
Personnel irrespectueux ! Hormis quel que exception Franchement, mon expérience avec le personnel de Saudia Airlines à l’aéroport de Djeddah a été catastrophique. Un niveau de désagrément rarement vu : attitude froide, méprisante, et aucun sens du service ! Le personnel quitte son poste d'enregistrement de bagage en laissant une file d'attente pleine et ne reviens plus !!!

Le pire reste le traitement réservé à certains passagers, notamment africains, qui m’a profondément choqué. On a clairement l’impression d’un manque total de respect, avec des comportements et des paroles qui frôlent (voire dépassent) les limites de l’acceptable. C’est humiliant et inadmissible en 2026.

Dans un aéroport international, on s’attend à un minimum de professionnalisme et d’humanité. Là, c’est tout l’inverse : aucune considération, aucune politesse, et une impression très marquée de discrimination.

Je déconseille fortement cette compagnie si vous tenez à être traité avec dignité. Une telle attitude ne devrait tout simplement pas exister dans le secteur du transport aérien.
translated_text: Disrespectful staff! Except for a few exceptions, frankly, my experience with Saudia Airlines staff at Jeddah airport was catastrophic. A level of unpleasantness rarely seen: cold attitude, contemptuous, and no sense of service! The staff leaves their baggage check-in post leaving a full queue and never returns!!! The worst is the treatment reserved for some passengers, especially Africans, which deeply shocked me. There is clearly a total lack of respect, with behaviors and words that border (or even exceed) the limits of acceptability. It is humiliating and unacceptable in 2026. In an international airport, one expects a minimum of professionalism and humanity. Here, it is the opposite: no consideration, no politeness, and a very marked impression of discrimination. I strongly discourage this company if you want to be treated with dignity. Such an attitude simply should not exist in the air transport sector.

raw_text:
Do not never ever consider to fly with Flynas My bag has been transferred from a Saudia flight ADD-JED flight SV3254 on March 18th. This bag has never been collected by me, because I never left the transit area at Jeddah Airport. After the booking JED-CAI has been done for the flight Flynas XY571 on 19.3.2026 the bag according to Saudia + Flynas counter at Jeddah transit area was with them and I should rest assured that the checked bag will be found at the airport in Cairo once landed. And exactly in Cairo the mess started: first at the terminal 1 hall 3 where all people were yelling and screaming for all missing bags including me. At some point after 2 hours somebody from Flynas representatives was telling me I have to take the airport shuttle bus on my own for going to the Terminal 2 to see if somebody from Saudia there was aware of my missing bag!!!!!!!!!! I came across some Saudia representative only when contacting the TOURIST POLICE, because some agent went to look for some Saudia agent who could assist and came outside into the arrivals zone to tell me my suitcase is still at Jeddah Airport and they will locate it and then send it to me in my hotel in Giza. No case has been started, only following reference number was given to me as per photo written on a piece of paper: 83104 OMDJEDSV Telephone numbers were given 01159110049 and also a private whatsapp which never respond my messages referring to Ms. MANAR. I tried to reach these phone numbers through the hotel owner where I am staying in Giza and he has been told he reached out of the working hours (in the morning) and told him to call the same number after 3 pm. He called for me again at 3.30 pm and has been told he has the wrong number and that I should go back from Giza to Cairo International Airport 1 (hour drive on my expenses!!!!!!) to find out what happened with my suitcase. Are you guys serious???? I mean as a Tour Manager | Tour guide | Tourist Operational Manager myself and former flight attendant, I think you have a huge problem in giving customer service in the aviation industry. I am wondering how the ICAO acts in countries like Saudi Arabia and Egypt at this point! Further I also I want to underlined that in my name there are 2 flights on Saudia International + 1 flight on Flynas International between March 10 and March 19, 2026. Is this the way Saudia + Flynas are able to assist their customers???? (Saudia owns Flynas for general information in case somebody working in one or another was not aware of that!!!) Try to make a slight effort and give me for the first time a timely manner professional answer by return.

PS: I try to submit this complaint on flynas website in their SYSTEM and I receive following: NO RESPONSE RECEIVED FROM SERVER - this flight company turns to be a joke in my eyes. I will publish this in the reviews and inform my Embassy about the matter because needless to say nobody of these "professionals" care about me having no clothes, no shoes, no toiletry since March 18th, 2026 (today is March 20th, 2026) I am still in my same clothes for 3 days in a row and no solutions and assistance are offered. Flynas is the worst flight company ever. Further no incident with reference number has been registered. They had only fill in forms in Arabic and the staff was not able to assist in ENGLISH. Shame on you, shame on you!!!!!! Is this the compassion the Ramadan | Eid teaches for??

raw_text:
Underrated Airlines with great Business Class experience The service onboard this leg was good, with a very attentive cabin crew. Despite not having smiles or striking conversation, she was observed to be attentive, and I felt very well taken care of by her. Although the service on board seemed to be taken from a page of the airline's SOP and executed by a very well-oiled crew, I could feel the attentiveness of the cabin crew working on my aisle. Throughout the flight, the cabin crew was kept busy with the different meal times. And when she walked by and saw that I was still awake, she offered to serve my light meal. After clearing my table, the same cabin crew came over to my seat with a tray of snacks, and at the same time, checked if I wanted any drinks to go along. During breakfast time, the cabin crew, seeing that I was awake, quickly heated up my breakfast order and served it after seeing I was settled down. The attentive cabin crew quickly cleared my table. We were presented with a retro white Saudia-branded amenity kit shortly after boarding. Inside the amenity kit, I found the Norwegian-branded Sprekenhus products such as lip balm, facial mist and hand cream, alongside eyemasks, dental kits and Saudia-branded socks.

Saudia houses all its 24 Business Class passengers in a single cabin onboard its B787-10 aircraft. Saudia B787 Business Class cabin looks clean and classy. The cream-coloured high-back seats bring out the black used in the framing of the IFE screen, while the dual-tone sand colour used in the inviting leather seat pays homage to the desert landscape in the kingdom, and the wood-grained finish on the seat's tables exudes a sense of luxury. The earth colour used in the carpet and the interior of the seat back throughout the cabin makes one feel relaxed. White walls and the bright lighting during boarding accentuate the cabin's clean look. There are three lavatories, one in the front of the aircraft and two at the back between the Business Class and Economy Class cabins. The dark brown used in the flooring and the cabinetry in the lavatory gave it a distinct, posh look. The lavatory was stocked with Saudia's in-house Eau de Parfum, hand lotion, and facial mist.

Business Class seats onboard Saudia B787-10 aircraft are arranged in a 1-2-1 configuration. The dual-tone sand-coloured leather seat has a generous seat pitch of 78” and measures 21” wide. The aisle side armrest can be lowered to give the seat a more generous width. Like most Business Class seats, these onboard Saudia B787 seats come in three standard configurations—Take-off/landing, lounge, and bed modes —which can be activated using the touchscreen seat control panel. The seat felt comfortable even in its most uncomfortable upright position for take-off/landing, thanks to the thick padding and the soft leather used in these seats. In bed mode, the seat can recline to 180°, turning it into a comfortable flatbed on which we had a great sleep during the flight. Despite not having any doors in these seats, I still find a good amount of privacy in bed mode thanks to the high back of the seat. The lounge mode sits between the upright and flatbed modes, which are angled to provide passenger comfort. I like how the angles in these seats can be further adjusted, giving passengers the freedom to customise the seat angle to their preferences.

I thought the storage in the Business Class seats onboard Saudia B787-10 aircraft was very generous. Right in the adjustable armrest on the aisle side of the seat lies a small compartment, large enough for the airlines to stow the provided headphones or bottled water. The main storage is found under the side table between the two seats (for those sitting in the middle couple seats) or at the window. There is one large compartment beneath the side table on the Business Class seat. The IFE controller and the charging outlets are placed above the side table at the seat. There is another storage area in the small cubby near the calf area inside the seat. I was also able to use the space under the footwell to place my carry-on. Saudia B787-10 aircraft Business Class is fitted with a large wooden table. It is even useful when stored under the IFE screen, with ⅔ of the bi-fold table sticking out, giving us a good amount of space to place our drinks. The table, when fully deployed, is rather sizeable and sturdy, which is perfect for meals or for doing some work. When it comes to illumination in the seat, aside from the overhead reading lights, airlines installed a smaller reading light. I like how the aircraft is fitted with air nozzles, giving us some freedom in adjusting the temperature in our seats.

Each Business Class seat is fitted with a large 18" HD personal TV. The picture quality is crisp and sharp, making watching shows on this TV pleasant. The IFE controllers Saudia uses resemble a smartphone, with most entertainment options controlled via touchscreen menus on the controller. BEYOND is Saudia's in-flight entertainment system, loaded with tons of entertainment options, from movies to TV shows to music. Saudia provides noise-cancelling headphones to all its Business Class passengers. Despite the ear cups' rectangular design, I still find these headphones comfortable to wear for a prolonged period, and they do a good job of blocking ambient noise. Despite offering complimentary Wi-Fi to Business Class passengers, I was not able to connect to the Wi-Fi on both legs of the flight on board Saudia B787 series aircraft.

Saudia offers a dine-on-demand concept, serving two meals for our 9-hour flight to and from Jeddah: a light meal service and a breakfast service. For the light meal, the three options that we had on the menu were Arabic Mezze, Chicken Satay and Lamb Kofta. I chose the chicken satay, which I found to be delicious. The chicken was tender, and the peanut satay sauce was flavourful. For breakfast, we could choose from the Saudia Sufrah set or from the à la carte menu. The à la carte menu breakfast came in two courses, and we were given three choices for both courses. For starters, we can choose between Greek Yoghurt, Fresh Fruit and Seared Halloumi; while the mains were a choice between Lamb Fettah, Organic eggs made onboard and Vanilla Pancakes. I opted for fresh fruit for starters and Lamb Fettah for mains. The fruits served were sweet and juicy, and the lamb was flavourful and well-spiced. There is a side dish of two pastry puffs that were stuffed with spinach. I am not a fan of these puffs due to personal preference.

raw_text:
Rude on every level Terrible experience, not worth the money saved on flying other reputable airlines. Staff is almost universally extremely rude, as if it’s part of their training (or the lack there of).
Starting from check-in staff: very rude, not even a smile or a greet. Refused to ship zamzam water despite going for umrah. When I asked to speak to the manager, she waved her hand and said “he’s over there go talk to him”
Staff facilitating boarding wasn’t any more friendly. A passenger was verifying if he was at the right gate. The employee responded “can’t you read the monitor?”
Boarding was extremely hectic and unorganized. The announced boarding was complete as passengers were continuing to roll in and struggling to find overhead space. Take of was delayed 45 minutes and landing was delayed an hour and a half.
The flight attendants were very rigid, unpleasant. I don’t recall anyone smiling throughout the trip.
I think vigorous training is extremely necessary if they want to stay in business

raw_text:
Comfortable Seats with Narrow Footwell but good service Saudia only uses one door for all passengers during boarding, which marred the boarding experience due to the long wait in line at the door. Service started from the mandatory welcome at the door during boarding, where we were welcomed onboard and guided to our seats. Once we were at our seats, the cabin crew working on our aisle would approach each passenger to serve the welcome drink, followed by a serving of Arabic coffee. Saudia offered their recently refreshed amenity kit from ELIE SAAB, which contains a selection of skincare products such as face mist, hand and body lotion, as well as Eau de Toilette on top of the usual dental kits, eye masks and socks. The cabin crew came across as warm and kept her smile throughout the flight, despite seeing how busy she was. After serving the welcoming drink, the Filipino cabin crew later would return to take our orders for meals and the time we wanted to have them. She would subsequently reappear after her meal order rounds with all the passengers on her aisle, with the distribution of amenity kits to all passengers. Despite the busyness, the cabin crew was able to pay attention to detail and still care for all the passengers under her charge.

Business Class cabin on Saudia's A330-343 looked cramp, with all 30 seats packed into a single cabin. However, the cabin's colour scheme and the white lighting used during boarding gave it a clean look. The dual-tone sand-coloured seats complemented the cream coloured seat shells well. The drape of black colour used in the IFE screen and the side of the seat gave the cabin a touch of elegance amidst a mildly coloured cabin. The wooden grain centre consoles between the seats, and the earthy brown carpet, exude a premium vibe. There is only one lavatory serving all 30 Business Class passengers, located in front of the plane. The wooden grain used for the flooring and the dark brown used for the cabinet panels gave the lavatory a premium look. Saudia stocks its Business Class lavatories with additional in-house amenities, such as Eau de Parfum, hand lotion, and facial mist, in addition to the usual hand wash.

Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats are arranged in a 2-2-2 configuration, each comes with a seat pitch of 60" and a width of 19". One of the armrests can be lowered to increase the seat width slightly in bed mode. The thick cushioning and soft leather on the Saudia A300-343 Business Class seats are comfortable to sit on, even in upright mode. The comfort of these Business Class seats is very well translated to a comfortable bed when reclined 180° into a flatbed. I find a good amount of privacy in bed mode, thanks to the high-shell-back cocoon design of the seats. However, the narrow footwell in these Business Class seats might be restrictive for passengers when the seat is in bed mode. In addition to the flatbed and upright modes, the seat also offers a more relaxing lounge mode, a mode between the upright and bed modes. We found additional buttons that allowed us to customise the seat back recline angle, the calf rest angle, and the amount of lumbar support to our preferred angles that improved the comfort of the seat further.

Storage was the biggest weakness in the Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats. The only space we can store anything is the small cubby hole in the inside armrest of the centre console. This space is so small that I can only stow my mobile phone, a couple of charging cables and my passport. While we could use the seat pocket situated next to the headrest, the shallow, slim design does not seem to allow me to stow anything. Due to the footwell's small design, the space beneath is so small that I can hardly place my shoes under the footwell, let alone a backpack. There are two tables in each Business Class seat. The main seat table is stored in the centre console between the two seats. This table is sizeable and sturdy offerring ample space for one to dig into the airline's delicious meals or work on a laptop. I like how Saudia uses the space between the centre console as a side table. When it comes to powering up our lifestyle devices, Saudia fitted two USB-A charging outlets on the side of the seats on the black side extension (the headphone jack and IFE controller are also found on this black extension), on top of the international power outlets between the two seats. Each Business Class seat is also fitted with a small reading light next to the headrest of the seat.

Saudia fitted a 17" touchscreen personal TV in every Business Class seat onboard their A330-343. These large TV screens deliver sharp picture quality, making watching the airline's entertainment very pleasant. Saudia's IFE controller resembles a mobile phone, with minimal buttons and selections are made on the device's touchscreen. Each Business Class passenger is provided with a noise-cancelling headphone by Saudia. BEYOND, Saudia's in-flight entertainment system, is very well stocked with movies and shows, ranging from Hollywood blockbusters to regional movies and shows. Saudia also offers its Business Class passengers 10MB of complimentary Wi-Fi, which is only good for browsing and uploading to social media. However, connecting to the in-flight Wi-Fi requires us to enter a long string of ticket numbers, and it would not work again after one's phone went into sleep mode.

Saudia catered with two meals for this 6 hour flight, consisting the main breakfast and the light dining meal. For breakfast, the Saudia Sufrah option comprises Banana and Date Pudding with accompanying side dishes and traditional Arabic bread. For starters, in the à la carte menu, we could choose from Smoked Salmon, Chia Seeds Pudding or Fresh Fruit. The mains were choices between Spiced Salmon Frittata, Scrambled or Pan Fried Eggs, and Hong Kong Toast. And for Light Dining, it was a choice of Arabic Mezze, Bocconcini Salad and Wagyu Beef Burger. I opted for the Saudia Sufrah for breakfast. I had no idea what the pudding was made from, which tasted a little sticky. But the flavours from the caramelised banana and the toasted nuts from the Banana and Date Pudding were delicious. The accompanying manakeesh topped with thyme sauce was refreshing. I opted the Wagyu beef burger from the light dining. The beef was juicy, and the caramelised onion was tasty. The chunky chips were fried to perfection and not soggy.
translated_text:
Comfortable Seats with Narrow Footwell but good service Saudia only uses one door for all passengers during boarding, which marred the boarding experience due to the long wait in line at the door. Service started from the mandatory welcome at the door during boarding, where we were welcomed onboard and guided to our seats. Once we were at our seats, the cabin crew working on our aisle would approach each passenger to serve the welcome drink, followed by a serving of Arabic coffee. Saudia offered their recently refreshed amenity kit from ELIE SAAB, which contains a selection of skincare products such as face mist, hand and body lotion, as well as Eau de Toilette on top of the usual dental kits, eye masks and socks. The cabin crew came across as warm and kept her smile throughout the flight, despite seeing how busy she was. After serving the welcoming drink, the Filipino cabin crew later would return to take our orders for meals and the time we wanted to have them. She would subsequently reappear after her meal order rounds with all the passengers on her aisle, with the distribution of amenity kits to all passengers. Despite the busyness, the cabin crew was able to pay attention to detail and still care for all the passengers under her charge.

Business Class cabin on Saudia's A330-343 looked cramp, with all 30 seats packed into a single cabin. However, the cabin's colour scheme and the white lighting used during boarding gave it a clean look. The dual-tone sand-coloured seats complemented the cream coloured seat shells well. The drape of black colour used in the IFE screen and the side of the seat gave the cabin a touch of elegance amidst a mildly coloured cabin. The wooden grain centre consoles between the seats, and the earthy brown carpet, exude a premium vibe. There is only one lavatory serving all 30 Business Class passengers, located in front of the plane. The wooden grain used for the flooring and the dark brown used for the cabinet panels gave the lavatory a premium look. Saudia stocks its Business Class lavatories with additional in-house amenities, such as Eau de Parfum, hand lotion, and facial mist, in addition to the usual hand wash.

Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats are arranged in a 2-2-2 configuration, each comes with a seat pitch of 60" and a width of 19". One of the armrests can be lowered to increase the seat width slightly in bed mode. The thick cushioning and soft leather on the Saudia A300-343 Business Class seats are comfortable to sit on, even in upright mode. The comfort of these Business Class seats is very well translated to a comfortable bed when reclined 180° into a flatbed. I find a good amount of privacy in bed mode, thanks to the high-shell-back cocoon design of the seats. However, the narrow footwell in these Business Class seats might be restrictive for passengers when the seat is in bed mode. In addition to the flatbed and upright modes, the seat also offers a more relaxing lounge mode, a mode between the upright and bed modes. We found additional buttons that allowed us to customise the seat back recline angle, the calf rest angle, and the amount of lumbar support to our preferred angles that improved the comfort of the seat further.

Storage was the biggest weakness in the Saudia A330-343 Business Class seats. The only space we can store anything is the small cubby hole in the inside armrest of the centre console. This space is so small that I can only stow my mobile phone, a couple of charging cables and my passport. While we could use the seat pocket situated next to the headrest, the shallow, slim design does not seem to allow me to stow anything. Due to the footwell's small design, the space beneath is so small that I can hardly place my shoes under the footwell, let alone a backpack. There are two tables in each Business Class seat. The main seat table is stored in the centre console between the two seats. This table is sizeable and sturdy offerring ample space for one to dig into the airline's delicious meals or work on a laptop. I like how Saudia uses the space between the centre console as a side table. When it comes to powering up our lifestyle devices, Saudia fitted two USB-A charging outlets on the side of the seats on the black side extension (the headphone jack and IFE controller are also found on this black extension), on top of the international power outlets between the two seats. Each Business Class seat is also fitted with a small reading light next to the headrest of the seat.

Saudia fitted a 17" touchscreen personal TV in every Business Class seat onboard their A330-343. These large TV screens deliver sharp picture quality, making watching the airline's entertainment very pleasant. Saudia's IFE controller resembles a mobile phone, with minimal buttons and selections are made on the device's touchscreen. Each Business Class passenger is provided with a noise-cancelling headphone by Saudia. BEYOND, Saudia's in-flight entertainment system, is very well stocked with movies and shows, ranging from Hollywood blockbusters to regional movies and shows. Saudia also offers its Business Class passengers 10MB of complimentary Wi-Fi, which is only good for browsing and uploading to social media. However, connecting to the in-flight Wi-Fi requires us to enter a long string of ticket numbers, and it would not work again after one's phone went into sleep mode.

Saudia catered with two meals for this 6 hour flight, consisting the main breakfast and the light dining meal. For breakfast, the Saudia Sufrah option comprises Banana and Date Pudding with accompanying side dishes and traditional Arabic bread. For starters, in the à la carte menu, we could choose from Smoked Salmon, Chia Seeds Pudding or Fresh Fruit. The mains were choices between Spiced Salmon Frittata, Scrambled or Pan Fried Eggs, and Hong Kong Toast. And for Light Dining, it was a choice of Arabic Mezze, Bocconcini Salad and Wagyu Beef Burger. I opted for the Saudia Sufrah for breakfast. I had no idea what the pudding was made from, which tasted a little sticky. But the flavours from the caramelised banana and the toasted nuts from the Banana and Date Pudding were delicious. The accompanying manakeesh topped with thyme sauce was refreshing. I opted the Wagyu beef burger from the light dining. The beef was juicy, and the caramelised onion was tasty. The chunky chips were fried to perfection and not soggy.

Worst Airline customer service ⭐ 1/5 – Worst Airline Experience Ever

Flynas is honestly the worst airline I have ever dealt with.

I had a flight from Madinah (MED) to Dubai (DXB), and due to ongoing regional tensions, the flight was cancelled. The worst part is the way they handled it.

They sent me an email asking me to check in — even though the flight was already cancelled. This shows complete mismanagement and lack of coordination.

I tried calling their call center countless times, but no one answered. Instead, I kept getting recorded Eid Mubarak messages and was never able to speak to an actual person.

Then I tried contacting them on WhatsApp — it’s completely useless. Just automated replies, no real support at all.

Finally, when I managed to process cancellation, they offered me only 1500 SAR refund, which is unacceptable considering the situation.

There is literally no proper customer service, no accountability, and no real human support available.

Strongly advise everyone: stay away from Flynas.
Save your money, time, and peace of mind.

Disappointing and unprofessional rude staff premium Eco not wort it I recently travelled with Flynas under booking reference M8ESSF, having upgraded to Premium Economy with the expectation of a smoother and more efficient airport experience. Unfortunately, the reality was the complete opposite.
At the check-in counter, the staff member handling our passports abruptly chose to assist another passenger who arrived after us, leaving us waiting without explanation. This raised serious concerns about fairness and professionalism in customer handling.
Despite holding Premium Economy tickets, we were not initially acknowledged as premium passengers. I had to repeatedly insist before we were assisted at the correct counter, which completely defeats the purpose of paying for an upgraded service.
The baggage handling process was equally frustrating. Our entitled additional luggage allowance was not properly recognised, and our Zamzam water was refused for check-in due to our transit through Riyadh. However, I personally observed other passengers in similar situations being allowed to check theirs in. This inconsistency is unacceptable and suggests a lack of standardised procedures.
Most concerning was the staff member’s dismissive attitude and lack of basic customer service. The overall conduct was unprofessional and far below what one would expect from an airline offering premium services.
In summary, this experience reflects poor service standards, inconsistent policy enforcement, and a disappointing lack of customer care. I would strongly caution others against paying extra for Premium services with this airline until these issues are properly addressed.

raw_text:
I booked a ticket through their official website and entered the passenger’s passport details correctly. Not “mostly correctly.” Not “close enough.” Correctly. Their system then decided, for reasons known only to itself, to save my details instead of the passenger’s and turn the whole booking into a mess from the start.

I noticed the issue early and tried to fix it immediately. I called them right away, but CSU was unavailable at the time. I then emailed them within about an hour of booking, asking them to correct the error before departure. They didn’t. So the problem was flagged early, reported early, and still left unresolved.

That’s what makes this experience so absurd.

The worst part was not the original system mistake. Systems fail. Fine. The real problem was what came after: delay, contradiction, and zero accountability. I followed up by phone, email, and WhatsApp. At one point, their own messages confirmed that the booking showed my name linked to another passenger’s Egyptian passport details. Read that again. Their own messages confirmed the data mismatch. Later, they also confirmed the booking was fully paid, no refund had been processed, no credit had been issued, and the case was still pending. Then, after all of that, they suddenly switched tone and acted like there was “no system error” and tried to hide behind fare rules and no-show policy.

So apparently the flynas process works like this:
their system creates the problem,
their support takes forever,
their channels give you different answers,
and then somehow you’re still expected to absorb the loss and thank them for the experience.

Amazing business model.

What really stood out was how exhausting they made everything. You don’t deal with one clear person who takes ownership. You get pushed from one channel to another, told to wait, told to follow up, told the case is pending, then told something completely different later. It felt less like customer service and more like being trapped in a badly written dark comedy where every new reply somehow makes the situation dumber.

And here’s my honest advice to anyone dealing with this company:
the second something goes wrong, get a case number.
Not later. Not after the fifth follow-up. Immediately. Get everything in writing. Keep screenshots. Keep emails. Keep call logs. And the moment you notice stalling, contradictions, or generic copy-paste responses, file a formal complaint and escalate it properly. Do not rely on verbal promises, and do not assume they’ll “sort it out.” Protect yourself early.

Because if this happened to me after entering the correct details and reporting the issue immediately, it can happen to anyone.

This was not just bad service. It was sloppy handling, repeated delay, contradictory communication, and a complete failure to take responsibility for a problem that started on their side.

Would I book with them again? Absolutely not.
Would I recommend them? Also no.
Would I recommend documenting everything if you already made the mistake of booking with them? 100%.

raw_text:
I am writing to formally complain about my recent experience with flynas, which caused significant inconvenience, financial loss, and distress to my family and me.

My husband booked a flight from Dubai to Riyadh during a period of regional instability. The flight was publicly announced by Flynas as operating; however, it was cancelled on the same day without any clear explanation, refund, or proper communication from customer service.

As a result, we were forced to book an alternative route from Dubai to Jeddah, followed by another flight to Riyadh. Upon arrival in Jeddah, we waited at the airport for several hours with our infant, only for the flight to be delayed repeatedly and eventually cancelled. We later discovered that many passengers had been experiencing the same issue throughout the day, with only one flight successfully departing.

Due to this situation, we were forced to leave the airport and book a hotel for the night at our own expense. The next day, during check-in, we were unexpectedly asked to pay an additional 300 SAR for our infant’s baggage, despite having checked in the same luggage the previous day without any issue. Furthermore, we were informed that we could not use our Flynas flight credit (points), even though these points were issued as a refund from a previous flight. The staff insisted on payment via Mada only and were uncooperative and dismissive.

This entire experience reflects:
•	Lack of transparency and reliability in flight operations
•	Failure to provide duty of care during delays and cancellations
•	Inconsistent and unfair baggage policy enforcement
•	Poor customer service and refusal to utilize valid flight credit

Traveling under these conditions with an infant made the situation even more difficult and unacceptable.

raw_text:
Threatened and Intimidated by Cabin Supervisor – Worst Airline Experience of My Life I am a British Pakistani citizen and a frequent traveller. What I experienced with Saudi Airlines was the worst airline experience of my life to my flight back from Karachi to London via Jeddah.

Shortly before take-off, the passenger seated next to me became severely unwell and was repeatedly vomiting. I politely informed the cabin crew and later calmly asked a supervisor whether another seat might be available. I fully understood the answer could be no.

Instead, the supervisor responded aggressively “Sit down on your seat or I kick you off from my flight, do you understand”. He then followed me to my seat, shouted loudly at me in front of other passengers, pointed his finger inches from my face, and repeatedly threatened to remove me from the aircraft, stating words to the effect of “This is my plane”. The behaviour lasted several minutes and was intimidating, threatening, and completely unprofessional. I remained quiet all this time when he was shouting on my face.

I genuinely feared for my safety. The incident was witnessed by many passengers, several of whom intervened and openly supported me. This incident was witnessed by many passengers, all of whom appeared shocked by his behaviour. When one passenger intervened and stated that his conduct was inappropriate, he responded by threatening to remove everyone from the plane if anyone complained or spoke up.
He then stopped the aircraft and attempted to have me removed from the flight.

Another crew member (Pakistani) approached me and asked me to apologise to the crew supervisor. I was deeply shocked by this request, as I had done nothing wrong. I refused.
I then decided to disembark voluntarily and took my hand luggage. At this point, several crew members, including two Pakistani staff, continued insisting that I apologise.

What happened next was extraordinary: numerous passengers who witnessed the incident stood up in my support, stating clearly that I should not be removed, nor asked to apologise. Several passengers stated they were prepared to leave the aircraft if I was forced off. Realising the seriousness of the situation, the crew supervisor changed his approach and allowed me to remain on board, and the aircraft departed.

After the flight, Saudi Airlines attempted to close my formal complaint with generic apology emails, provided no investigation findings, no accountability, and no compensation, despite the seriousness of the incident.

This experience left me humiliated, distressed, and psychologically shaken. It also raises serious concerns about passenger safety, staff conduct, and how complaints are handled. I am particularly troubled by whether such treatment is directed more commonly at Pakistani or South Asian passengers.

I will never fly with Saudi Airlines again and strongly advise others to think carefully before doing so.

The airlines was known for its luxury and generosity.
I travelled on 11th Feb from Jeddah to Bangalore.

At the check in counter
1. Ground staff guiding people are very very very rude. They scold people if we ask twice.

Worst board pass and security checks ever I had one of the worst airline experiences while traveling from Madina to Dhaka with Saudi Airlines.

At the airport security check, one staff member kept sending me back and forth repeatedly — at least five times — without giving any clear explanation. The behavior was extremely unprofessional and rude. He was also speaking badly to other passengers who were trying to board the flight.

Because of this unnecessary situation, I almost missed my flight and felt very frustrated and stressed.

I eventually went to the complaint department, and thankfully Mr. Adel helped resolve the issue. I appreciate his assistance and professionalism in handling the situation.

However, the overall experience was very disappointing, and it will probably be the last time I travel with Saudi Airlines. I will also be sending a formal email to the airline about this incident

Amazing cabin crew I recently had a flight with Flynas and overall it was a pleasant experience. The check-in process was smooth and efficient. I informed the staff that I had a connecting flight, and they were attentive enough to make sure my suitcases would be handled with priority, which I really appreciated.

At the lounge, the buffet was decent, although the variety of food was somewhat limited. I tried the rice with butter chicken sauce, which was actually quite good.

During boarding, flight attendant Lamees welcomed us with a warm smile. She was friendly, helpful, and very professional. She carefully checked boarding passes to make sure every passenger was seated correctly, showing great attention to detail. Throughout the flight, she kept passengers informed about the weather and possible turbulence, which made the journey more comfortable and reassuring.

Flynas is truly lucky to have someone like Lamees as part of their cabin crew. Her professionalism and positive attitude made the flight experience even better.

The worst flight company ever I had a very disappointing experience with Flynas, and it will be my last time traveling with them.

At Amman Airport, on my return to Jeddah, I was stopped by an employee, Nada Rawashdeh, for extra baggage. Although I had already purchased 30 kg, I was forced to pay an additional 60 JD. I followed the rules, but the way I was treated was unacceptable—especially when another passenger with more excess weight was allowed to pass without issue.

When I asked for clarification, she responded rudely, saying “I am Hurra,” implying it was her personal decision. Her attitude was dismissive and unprofessional. To make matters worse, her supervisor handled the situation sarcastically and supported her behavior.

This lack of fairness and professionalism is unacceptable. I will not be flying with Flynas again.

raw_text:
Extremely Disrespectful Treatment of Umrah Passengers Our experience with Flynas was one of the worst travel experiences our family has ever faced.

My parents were traveling for Umrah from Lahore to Jeddah with a connection in King Khalid International Airport. Their flight from Riyadh to King Abdulaziz International Airport was scheduled for 10:10 AM.

This was their first international trip. They were already standing in the boarding line while other passengers were boarding. When their turn came, a staff member suddenly stopped them and told them the plane had already left.

The most shocking part was the behavior of the staff. Instead of helping two confused Umrah passengers, the staff spoke harshly and showed no respect or guidance.

Because of this situation, my parents were forced to purchase another ticket costing 400 Saudi Riyals just to continue their journey.

This happened during Ramadan while they were fasting. Even on the later flight they were not given even a simple bottle of water for sehri. That level of carelessness toward passengers, especially Umrah travelers, is extremely disappointing.

They were also left stressed and confused about their luggage because no proper information was given about where their bags were.

Air travel mistakes can happen, but rude treatment and lack of basic humanity should never happen. Passengers deserve respect, especially elderly travelers visiting for a religious journey.

I hope the airline seriously improves its staff behavior and passenger support.

Broken Verification System and No Real Customer Support One of the worst customer service experiences I’ve ever had.

Booking a ticket is easy—you can receive an OTP via SMS or email. However, the system becomes completely frustrating when you need to modify or cancel your booking.

In my case, I never received the OTP via SMS, so I used the email option to complete my purchase. But later, when I wanted to cancel the ticket, the system only allowed SMS verification—which I still could not receive. This made cancellation impossible.

I then tried their virtual agent and provided all required details, but again I was asked for the SMS OTP used during booking. The issue is that their system assumes I used SMS (because I tried it first), even though I actually completed the process via email. So again, no solution.

Calling customer service was equally disappointing. The representative said they had no authority to help and advised me to send an email, with a response time of 5–6 weeks—while my flight is in just 2 weeks.

This is an extremely frustrating and poorly designed system. It feels like everything works smoothly until they receive your money, but after that, you’re left without real support.

I strongly advise others to be cautious when booking, especially if flexibility or support might be needed later.

The treatment was extremely poor and unprofessional, both from the company and the staff.

I bought a ticket on Flynas through the Turkish Pegasus app. The flight details only showed "operated by Flynas" during the purchase process. The employee refused to provide any service for my Flynas ticket simply because I bought it through an intermediary who was no better than them. The ticket was with Flynas, the flight was "operated by Flynas," yet she told me it wasn't their business, that it was a joint venture, and so on, using technical jargon I had no knowledge of. When it comes to collecting money, all the apps work, but when it comes to providing service, they all abandon you, including Flynas. They force you to buy extra baggage at the airport to pay more. It's simply taking people's money unjustly. May God hold them accountable.

raw_text:
Unfortunately, I had a very unpleasant experience on Flynas flight XY033 from Riyadh to Jeddah.

While boarding, I was placing my bag in the overhead compartment when a flight attendant (named Hajar, a Tunisian national) asked me to wait. Later, when I placed my bag in the overhead compartment, she spoke to me rudely and loudly, saying, "Everyone on the plane should be respectful, or I'll have you removed." She also accused me of being disrespectful in front of the other passengers, which caused me considerable embarrassment.

I then spoke with another flight attendant named Yazid, who was supposed to handle the situation professionally and calm things down. However, he refused to give me the flight attendant's name and even asked her to remove the name tag from her jacket so I wouldn't recognize her. I was able to identify her myself, though.

This was a terrible experience in terms of customer service. I hope your staff receive better training on communication skills, respect for passengers, and handling situations with wisdom and professionalism, befitting the company's reputation and customer comfort. I also hope that appropriate action will be taken regarding this incident.

The airline canceled the flight and only offered a travel credit, which I do not accept. I have clearly requested a full refund to my original payment method, as the cancellation was initiated by the airline.

In addition, I have attempted to contact Flyadeal multiple times via email and phone, but I have received no response. This lack of communication is highly unprofessional and unacceptable.

Furthermore, Flyadeal publicly stated on their official X account that all flights are canceled until March 31. However, when searching on their website & skyScanner & google the same flights still appear available for booking!!!! How??????

Moreover, I was able to issue a boarding pass for this flight. This raises serious concerns — how is it possible to issue a boarding pass for a canceled flight?

I also purchased travel insurance for this trip, which I paid for, and it has not been refunded. This adds to my financial loss due to the airline’s cancellation.

This situation is confusing, inconsistent, and potentially misleading to customers.

Due to this experience, I no longer wish to travel with this airline and request your intervention to ensure that my full refund, including any associated costs, is processed

raw_text:
Terrible customer service from Saudia Dear Saudia,
I had a ticket booked for the SV1023 flight (with Saudia) from Riyadh to Jeddah on the 19th December, to fly at 09:00 am. I arrived at Riyadh airport by 7 a.m, checked in, and went through security. While in the departure area, I noticed the flight was not mentioned on any screen until around 08.30 when it was listed as delayed and continued to be re-listed as delayed at 11.00 am, 12:00 p.m, then 13:00 p.m. Eventually, a gate was provided but no staff to help explain the situation- a theme consistent throughout the day. Without even a hint of customer service, I continued to wait without any information. The few staff who did wander past were utterly unhelpful, offering merely vague responses. Meanwhile, other airlines, including Flynas and Flyadeal, operated normally, while Saudia's passengers were stranded without any accountability from the company.

After more than 12 hours, I received a message, again, no information from any staff (terrible customer service), that our flight was cancelled. Collecting my luggage took another hour of chaotic searching, again, with no Saudia staff present (was it a spontaneous holiday for them perhaps?)

After leaving the airport and going back to the check-in area, I finally found a customer service desk. There, a massive queue had built up, but I was told to leave as no one was going to receive ANY help unless they had a connecting journey. Apparently, the cause of the delay was said to be “natural causes", therefore making Saudia completely absolved of liability!

What makes this even worse is that Saudia’s own Customer Rights Policy states: 1 hour delay → refreshments. 2 hours → a meal. 6 hours → 150% ticket refund, rebooking, and hotel accommodation, etc. I did not receive any of this as the delay was apparently due to "natural causes". Instead, I was dismissed to “go home and wait.” This experience revealed that Saudia is an airline built on empty promises and a lack of accountability. This terrible display of poor customer service and hospitality is appalling.

raw_text:
Extremely poor Quality of service I had an extremely disappointing experience with Saudi Airlines. On my recent flights between Dhaka, Jeddah, and Madinah, the cabin crew were inattentive, dismissive, and often more engaged in personal conversations than serving passengers. Requests for assistance were met with arrogance, and during a three‑hour delay, no proper information was provided. On an eight‑hour flight, only one meal was served, after which attendants were largely unavailable.

What’s most concerning is the disrespectful and discriminatory attitude shown toward Bangladeshi passengers, who were treated as though they had no right to basic courtesy. This level of service is unacceptable, and I strongly urge the airline to improve its standards and ensure fair treatment for all travelers.

I will never fly with Saudi Arabian Airlines again, and I strongly advise others to stay away. This airline is a complete mess.

I hope they read my comment and understand what their problem is. Good luck.

An airline that allows passengers’ belongings to be broken into, stolen from, and then refuses responsibility shows a shocking lack of integrity and respect.

Zero customer service. Zero accountability. Trust destroyed beyond repair.

raw_text:
This was one of the worst airline experiences of my life. My Flynas flight from Madinah was delayed by around 2 hours. We were clearly told that Sahoor would be provided, but that promise was completely broken. Instead of food, they gave passengers only water during Sahoor time in Ramadan. This was extremely disrespectful and unacceptable.

Because of this delay, I missed my connecting flight from Jeddah to Karachi. Flynas then placed us in a hotel, but the food quality was pathetic and far below basic standards.

After suffering a delay of nearly 24 hours, we finally boarded the next flight from Jeddah to Karachi. When we arrived, the situation became even worse: baggage was missing for around 30 passengers, including myself. Not a single bag arrived. No clear explanation, no proper assistance, and no accountability.

This airline showed:
• Zero care for passengers
• Poor communication
• Broken promises
• Bad food arrangements
• Total failure in baggage handling

I strongly do NOT recommend Flynas to anyone, especially for connecting or international flights. Save yourself the stress and choose another airline if you value your time, dignity, and belongings.

Terrible and Worst experience I had an extremely disappointing experience with the check-in services of Saudi Airlines. I was made to wait in the check-in queue for over two hours due to clear miscommunication and poor coordination among the staff.

The ground team appeared unprepared to handle the rush and showed no willingness to take responsibility. The repeated response from the staff was simply, “We don’t care, we are working here,” delivered in a rude and unacceptable manner.

This level of behavior and lack of customer service is highly unprofessional. I have traveled extensively with several international airlines, yet I have never encountered such a chaotic, poorly managed, and discourteous experience.

Overall, this was a pathetic and disastrous experience. I would not recommend this airline to others. I am attaching a few images for reference, which clearly show the uncontrolled rush and absence of proper queue management.

raw_text:
I arrived at the airport, and while checking in, they told me my seat had changed.

I explained that I had booked a specific seat and paid extra for it.

The response was: “The plane has changed, and we can’t return you to your seat.”

I said, “Okay… at least refund my seat selection fee.”

They said I had to contact customer service by phone, and that they couldn’t help me at the airport.

I did call, and they opened a “request” for me.

They said if I didn’t receive a refund within 15 business days, I should contact them.

Of course, I didn’t receive one.

I called to follow up… and they said there was no record of any request!

I was transferred to a supervisor, waited, and wasted time… and now, three months later, nothing has been refunded, and I’m certain it’s lost.

And today, a new situation:

I need to cancel a flight due to an emergency.

They said there’s a cancellation fee—and I agreed because it’s stated in the terms and conditions.

I paid online, and the amount was deducted… immediately afterward, a message appeared: “An error occurred on the page.”

I called. They took all my information, and then the employee said, “I’m sorry, dear sister, I can’t help you… Please file a complaint through the website.”

I asked myself: If the employee can’t help, how can an automated system?

I tried to file a complaint… The website kept freezing, crashing, and taking me back to the beginning.

And honestly?

No matter how much I try, I’m certain I’ll probably never get my rights back.

raw_text:
⭐☆☆☆☆

Very disappointing rules and customer service experience.

I booked a flight for almost 300 SAR and later needed to change the flight time to another option with the same price. Surprisingly, I was asked to pay nearly 250 SAR just to make the change, which simply does not make sense.

There were clearly free seats available, yet the airline preferred to enforce rigid and unreasonable rules instead of helping the customer. Many other airlines handle changes much more flexibly, especially when no price difference exists.

As someone who travels frequently, I know this process can be handled easily. Unfortunately, the lack of flexibility and customer support makes this a frustrating experience. The company should reconsider its policies and learn from other airlines on how to better support their passengers.

raw_text: By far the worst experience I’ve ever had with an Airline. A group of 6 of us had booked a weekend trip to Jazan from Riyadh. We arrived at RUH domestic terminal at roughly 4:30am on Friday the 20th of February ready to check in and board our flight (online check in wasn’t working on any browser or on the flyadeal app). We waited in line and walked up to the front desk and said we were wanting to check in to our 6:05am flight to Jazan. We were then informed that the flight was overbooked and asked to move aside and wait to speak to the manager/help desk. We were told that between Saudia and flyadeal, the flight we had paid for and booked over a week or so earlier had been overbooked by 12-14 passengers. There was no attempt to contact any of us before we got to the airport so were completely caught off guard by the situation. The flyadeal employee gave us three options, either a refund and to get on a later flight, something like 7:40pm, we could be provided with a hotel room or we could receive a “double refund” off our flight. We all decided to take the double refund option and the employee said she had put a reference next to all of our names (that we had written on a piece of paper for her) so that the process would run smoothly. Instead of being able to process this is in person with an employee, we were asked to go through the help centre online and fill out a form. The flyadeal employee informed us that we would be able to complete this form at anytime, that it would not be problem. Since then the customer support email thread included shows a clear misunderstanding and avoidance of responsibility. The support system makes it extremely difficult to have a clear line of communication and to gain some sort of understanding or traction. So we showed up on time, got denied access to our flight, got promised double our money for the flight and have received nothing but being told we’re wrong. Completely unacceptable, extremely frustrating and has left no option other than to complain.
translated_text: By far the worst experience I’ve ever had with an Airline. A group of 6 of us had booked a weekend trip to Jazan from Riyadh. We arrived at RUH domestic terminal at roughly 4:30am on Friday the 20th of February ready to check in and board our flight (online check in wasn’t working on any browser or on the flyadeal app). We waited in line and walked up to the front desk and said we were wanting to check in to our 6:05am flight to Jazan. We were then informed that the flight was overbooked and asked to move aside and wait to speak to the manager/help desk. We were told that between Saudia and flyadeal, the flight we had paid for and booked over a week or so earlier had been overbooked by 12-14 passengers. There was no attempt to contact any of us before we got to the airport so were completely caught off guard by the situation. The flyadeal employee gave us three options, either a refund and to get on a later flight, something like 7:40pm, we could be provided with a hotel room or we could receive a “double refund” off our flight. We all decided to take the double refund option and the employee said she had put a reference next to all of our names (that we had written on a piece of paper for her) so that the process would run smoothly. Instead of being able to process this is in person with an employee, we were asked to go through the help centre online and fill out a form. The flyadeal employee informed us that we would be able to complete this form at anytime, that it would not be problem. Since then the customer support email thread included shows a clear misunderstanding and avoidance of responsibility. The support system makes it extremely difficult to have a clear line of communication and to gain some sort of understanding or traction. So we showed up on time, got denied access to our flight, got promised double our money for the flight and have received nothing but being told we’re wrong. Completely unacceptable, extremely frustrating and has left no option other than to complain.

raw_text: I've had the worst experience on my return flight from Jeddah to Dhaka on 21st February,2026. Our flight SV802 was scheduled to depart at 3.30PM, during boarding they took us in front of the plane and came back to the gate and dropped us off. Without giving us any information regarding the situation. Nobody from Saudi airlines was there. We saw on the screen that our flight is rescheduled to 10PM. Delays can happen there's no issue in it. My issue is NOBODY informed us anything. It is Ramadan, we were all fasting that day. They didn't give us any necessary facilities to support during this delay. After an hour I saw one staff from their airlines and asked him what are they going to do as the flight is delayed more than 6 hours. According to GACA they should be giving us accomodations and good amount of food to sustain. He tried to bribe me by saying he will manage hotel for only my husband and me and then walked away as I was not convinced. After a while, 4 of them were standing together and few of us again went to them to ask about the update of our compensation. They shut me down by saying the settlement will be done later. Guess what the settlement was! A very thin box of rice and meat and a very little bottle of water to break our fast. That's it! They literally placed a carton box filled with our meals on the floor and without handing it to each customer, they just went away. Like we are animals! Everything they did during the delay only showed how disrespectful and unprofessional they can be. I asked where can I file complaints against them, they didn't tell me. Pretended not to know English. I know this review won't matter to your airlines, but know this, one day you will suffer for this. The person who tried to bribe us with hotel, the person who shut us down for asking about our basic rights, the person who gave us that little amount of food during iftar- I hope you guys face the same humiliation one day.

raw_text:
Strong Value in Business Class — With WiFi Caveats First time flying the airline and traveled from Mumbai to Jeddah and then Jeddah to JFK.

I actually did the Saudia stopover and the visa process as part of purchasing the ticket on Saudia was seamless and easy.

The Mumbai Jeddah flight was an older aircraft so the business cabin was 2-2-2 meaning if you were in the window seat, you had to jump over the person next to you. No WiFi and their approach to meal service sucked - I was in row 1 and they already ran out of my preference. That said it is a 5 hour flight and for that purpose it was fine.

The Jeddah JFk is a modern aircraft and the experience was generally good, at least for th price. Arrival in Jeddah had its own business class entrance and security line and was gas and seamless. There is a nice lounge as well although it was packed.

This flight has both first and business with first being private suites and business being 1-2-1 angled seats with full 180 lie flat. The cabin is modern, and they provided a pillow, blanket and toiletry bag. The menu offers lots of à la carte options and you can dine when you please. The flight was quiet and respectful and I slept most of it. The bathrooms seemed like they were cleaned throughout the flight.

My gripes:
WiFi coverage was not only slow and terrible, but the business code worked for a very short time and it was $500 for 200 MB otherwise. You can’t work on this flight. I tried to order two small items from the al a carte menu and i got pushback!! And of course, the flight is dry. Not a big deal to me but worth mentioning it.

In my case this flight was half the price of the others so it was good value, but I can’t see it’s competing with Quatar for example.

Bad experience I recently travelled from Jeddah to Lahore on Saudia Airlines (Flight SV732), and unfortunately, my experience was not satisfactory. The aircraft interior felt quite outdated, and the seating was very congested, making the journey uncomfortable, especially for a long-haul flight.

Additionally, the in-flight entertainment system was not functioning, as the TV screens were not working, which left passengers without any form of engagement during the trip. The quality of the food served on board was also below expectations.

I hope the airline considers upgrading its aircraft, improving seat comfort, and ensuring that entertainment systems are fully operational to provide a better travel experience for passengers in the future.

raw_text:
Absolutely disgraceful experience with flyadeal.
This will be my first and last flight with this airline. I deeply regret not checking reviews before booking, I booked first and only later discovered how consistently terrible their staff behavior is. Now I understand why.

This happened at boarding for Flight F3-829 on 14 Feb 2026. The ground staff member I dealt with, Mohammad Tariq (if that was his name), was shockingly rude, arrogant, and completely unfit for a customer-facing role. No manners, no respect, no professionalism, just attitude.

Mate, you are paid to provide a service. Your salary comes from passengers’ ticket money. Acting like customers are beneath you is unacceptable. You are not doing us a favor ,this is literally your job.

The language issue made things even worse. If I don’t understand your Arabic, that is not my problem, and it certainly doesn’t give you the right to be rude. I spoke calmly and clearly in English, and instead of helping, your behavior became even more disrespectful. If you work for an airline that serves international passengers, English communication is a basic requirement. If you can’t speak or understand English properly, or can’t do so politely, then you should not be working at boarding.

The entire interaction felt hostile, dismissive, and embarrassing. Boarding staff are meant to manage passengers professionally, not bully them, talk down to them, or make the situation worse.

This single experience was enough to permanently lose me as a customer. I will never fly flyadeal again, and I will actively warn others to avoid this airline unless they enjoy being treated with disrespect.

Terrible staff. Toxic attitude. Zero customer service. Avoid.

raw_text:
1★ Review – Unauthorized Cancellation & No Proper Resolution (PNR ZRQEHY)

I rarely write negative reviews, but this experience has been extremely frustrating.

Booking reference: ZRQEHY

I had a JED–DXB flight booked for 579 SAR. Due to a separate segment cancellation on the same booking, I requested a refund only for the canceled portion.

Instead, flynas refunded the entire booking and canceled my JED–DXB ticket without my request or consent.

I never authorized cancellation of that flight.

Since then:

The same flight has increased in price to 709.99 SAR

I have contacted customer service five times

My issue continues to receive generic or related responses

The core problem (unauthorized cancellation of JED–DXB) remains unresolved

Each delay has caused the fare to increase further.

I am not asking for special treatment—only for the correction of an error that was not my fault:

Either reinstate the original ticket

Or allow a repurchase at the original fare

Or refund the fare difference

So far, there has been no proper resolution.


I will now be filing a formal complaint with the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA) in Saudi Arabia, as well as documenting this case publicly.

I hope flynas chooses to correct this before further escalation becomes necessary.

☆☆☆☆ One-Star Review – Unauthorized Cancellation and No Resolution (Booking Number ZRQEHY)

I rarely write negative reviews, but my experience with flynas was extremely disappointing.

Booking Number: ZRQEHY

I had a booking for a Jeddah-Dubai flight for SAR 579. Due to the cancellation of another part of the same booking, I requested a refund for only the canceled portion.

Instead, flynas refunded my entire booking and canceled my Jeddah-Dubai flight without my request or consent.

I did not agree to the cancellation of this flight.

Since then:

The price of the same flight has increased to SAR 709.99.

I have contacted customer service five times.

I receive generic or irrelevant responses.

The core issue remains unresolved.

Every delay results in an increased ticket price.

I am not asking for special treatment, only for the correction of an error for which I am not responsible:

Either reactivate my original ticket.

Or allow me to rebook at the original price.

Or provide a full refund of the price difference.

So far, no effective solution has been offered.

I will be filing a formal complaint with the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA) and will also publicly document this issue.

I hope flynas will resolve the problem before I escalate it officially.

This review concerns a live animal transported under Saudi Cargo operations on Flight SV302 (03 January 2026).

The animal was medically cleared prior to travel and later sustained fatal trauma while under custody. No written incident explanation has been provided despite documented medical findings.

The case has been escalated to the relevant aviation authorities for review.

Passengers transporting live animals should ensure full understanding of handling standards and escalation procedures.
